markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 201 **Aspects** Beast and Celestial --- An _agathion raiment_ aids its wearer in finding diplomatic solutions to conflicts when possible, fighting evil directly when it’s not, and guiding others along the path to redemption. An [agathion](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=216) created five _agathion raiments_ and granted them to champions of great valor millennia ago. Since then, virtuous souls of various skill sets have assembled _agathion raiments_. This set functions only for [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) creatures. --- **Set Items**: [Flask of Fellowship](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1056) (level 2), [Aeon Stone (Envisioning)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3055) (level 8), [Messenger's Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=448) (level 9), [Holy Chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3273) (level 13), [Headwrap of Wisdom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3011) (level 17) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Word of Faith](/Relics.aspx?ID=15) - 2: [Feral Claws](/Relics.aspx?ID=9) - 3: [Righteous Call](/Relics.aspx?ID=17) - 4: [Form of Fury](/Relics.aspx?ID=10) (usually canine or cat) - 5: [Angelic Vessel](/Relics.aspx?ID=18) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items_: Word of faith restores 1d10 Hit Points per relic level and reduces the target's [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) value by 2.
  • _Three Items_: The claws you gain from feral claws are [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) and deal 1d4 spirit damage.
  • _Four Items_: You and your allies affected by righteous call deal 1d4 additional spirit damage with successful Strikes, rather than none or 1. This 1d4 stacks with that from your feral claws and damage from the [_holy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) rune effect you gain from righteous call.
  • _Five Items_: In your form of fury, you have [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) and gain 20 additional temporary Hit Points, but you have weakness 5 to unholy. You don't need to change your size to gain the benefits, other than reach, of a higher-level battle form. Also, your unarmed attacks from this form have the _holy_ weapon property rune.
name: Agathion Raiment

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 201 **Aspects** Life and Mind --- Many [wizards](/Classes.aspx?ID=12) have enhanced their spellcasting capabilities with an _archmage's regalia_. Legend tells of such a relic set that belonged to the wizard-king Nex, with powers far beyond that of a typical relic. Although some believe Nex took his regalia with him when he disappeared, several credible reports of items matching their descriptions have surfaced over the years. No one has yet managed to track down all five pieces, and it's possible some have been destroyed or otherwise lost. --- **Set Items**: [Mage's Hat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3094) (level 3), [Endless Grimoire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=992) (level 6), [Accolade Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3054) (level 10), [Bands of Force (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3058) (level 14), [Greater Staff of Arcane Might](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3038) (level 16) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Repository of Knowledge](/Relics.aspx?ID=51) - 2: [Bestow Life](/Relics.aspx?ID=43) - 3: [Psychic Scream](/Relics.aspx?ID=53) - 4: [Vitality Siphon](/Relics.aspx?ID=47) - 5: [Mental Bastion](/Relics.aspx?ID=54) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items:_ The repository of knowledge gift grants you one additional Lore skill of the GM’s choice. This skill advances as other skills from the gift do.
  • _Three Items:_ When you use your bestow life gift, if the animated object’s level is no greater than your level – 4, you can make it a minion with the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait.
  • _Four Items:_ The psychic scream gift creates a 30-foot‑radius [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385).
  • _Five Items:_ Treat the outcomes of skill checks you roll to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2366), and [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) as one degree of success better.
name: Archmage's Regalia

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 202 **Aspects** Emotion (pride) and Luck --- A _duelist's blazon_ set relic was once used by a legendary swordmaster, absorbing their verve, tenacity, and daring. Though each set contains the same items, they all have unique styles, from the elegant, rose-themed decor of the blazon of Taldan [swashbuckler](/Classes.aspx?ID=15) Miralla d'Asivran to the spiked sword and attire of Kuthonite maestro duelist Essinte Zaim. Duelists have been known to challenge one another over the set's components. --- **Set Items**: [+1 rapier](/Equipment.aspx?ID=380) (level 2), [Bravery Baldric (Fleet)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2309) (level 5), [Winged Sandals](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3119) (level 10), [Invisible Chain Shirt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1063) (level 13), [Mercurial Mantle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1067) (level 18) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Dominant Emotion](/Relics.aspx?ID=91) - 2: [Whammy](/Relics.aspx?ID=99) - 3: [Steal Luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=101) - 4: [Emotion Conduit](/Relics.aspx?ID=94) - 5: [Against All Odds](/Relics.aspx?ID=102) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items_: You can use dominant emotion at will.
  • _Three Items_: You can use whammy one additional time per day, but a creature affected by it is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
  • _Four Items_: When you use steal luck and get a success or critical success, you're [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) until the end of your next turn.
  • _Five Items_: If dominant emotion or emotion conduit [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) an effect, the target gains a brief surge of luck in the form of a d20 they can add to another single d20 roll, taking the highest result. Until used, this die remains with the target until their next daily preparations, and the target can't gain another die from this effect until after that time. Using the die is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.
name: Duelist's Blazon

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 202 **Aspects** Death and Shadow --- Several notable [assassins](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=48), [thieves](/Rackets.aspx?ID=3), and others who work from the shadows have used an _infiltration garb_ over the centuries. The nature of these professions means that such sets are often separated from their audacious users. Due to their association with subterfuge and assassins, sets of infiltration garbs have grim reputations. However, not all wielders use their powers maliciously. Notably, the faithful of Kelinahat, empyreal lord of spies, stealth, and intelligence gathering, view these relics as sacred tools in the fight against evil. --- **Set Items**: [Masquerade Scarf](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3095) (level 2), [Obsidian Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3097) (level 5), [Clandestine Cloak (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3067) (level 10), [Boots of Bounding (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=412) (level 14), [Ring of Maniacal Devices (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=457) (level 18) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Obscure](/Relics.aspx?ID=62) - 2: [Death Gaze](/Relics.aspx?ID=19) - 3: [Dark Roads](/Relics.aspx?ID=65) - 4: [Tide of Death](/Relics.aspx?ID=23) - 5: [Grim Specter](/Relics.aspx?ID=24) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items_: The relic set grants you a +1 status bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks you attempt in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408).
  • _Three Items_: You can use death gaze to deal positive damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160).
  • _Four Items_: You can use dark roads to teleport up to 60 feet.
  • _Five Items_: When you use tide of death, you can target [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) and yourself with [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) energy, restoring Hit Points to you (if vitality energy heals you) and damaging undead harmed by vitality energy.
name: Infiltration Garb

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 202 **Aspects** Emotion (dedication) and Life --- According to tales, some of the first [monks](/Classes.aspx?ID=8) to assemble the austere _monk's attire_ were servants of [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10). This deity is a symbol of devotion and self-discovery through one's understanding of body, mind, and soul. _Monk's attire_ resonates with Irori's dedication, focusing on the highest gain from minimal acquisition, eschewing material objects. Legend holds Irori did so, and that some famous _monk's attire_ sets include a scrap of Irori's original possessions. Other monastic orders have similar traditions of minimal personal effects, with an emphasis on spiritual focus, practicality, and self-defense. --- **Set Items**: [Handwraps of Mighty Blows (+1)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441) (level 2), [+1 explorer's clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) (level 5) or [Bands of Force](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3058) (level 8), [Mask of Uncanny Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2326) (level 11) or [Mala Beads of Foresight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2325) (level 11) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Overflowing Life](/Relics.aspx?ID=45) - 2: [Dominant Emotion](/Relics.aspx?ID=91) - 3: [Inextinguishable](/Relics.aspx?ID=46) - 3 (one 13th-level or higher item): [Emotion Conduit](/Relics.aspx?ID=94) - 3 (one 17th-level or higher item): [Quickening Emotion](/Relics.aspx?ID=96) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items_: The item bonus from overflowing life equals the relic's level.
  • _Three Items_: Any mental effect harmful to you triggers dominant emotion, which can counteract such effects.
  • _Three Items (one 13th-level or higher item)_: Treat the outcome of your recovery checks as one step better.
  • _Three Items (one 17th-level or higher item)_: If dominant emotion or emotion conduit [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) an effect, the target regains 5d10+5 Hit Points and gains a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects for 10 minutes.
name: Monk's Attire

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 202 **Aspects** Life and Time --- [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) devoted followers vow to seek out and destroy [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) wherever they can be found, a task made more urgent with the rise of Tar-Baphon. Though the Whispering Tyrant was prevented from taking over Golarion, his forces are still massing. Undead unaffiliated with the [lich](/Monsters.aspx?ID=282) represent an unacceptable perversion of the cycle of life and death to Pharasmins, and permitting such creatures to exist is anathema to her. To aid her faithful in their sacred duty to send the dead where they belong, Pharasmin priests developed this item set. While using _Pharasma's command_, you must abide by her [edicts and anathema](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) or else the relic ceases to function for you until you atone. This set doesn't function for undead beings. --- **Set Items**: [+1 dagger](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) (level 2), [Symbol of Conflict](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3111) (level 4), [Wand of Overflowing Life (3rd-Rank Spell)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3426) (level 9), [Devoted Vestments](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3077) (level 11) or [Cloister Robe (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2321) (level 15), [Staff of Healing (True)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3042) (level 16) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Healing Wave](/Relics.aspx?ID=44) - 2: [Sands of the Hourglass](/Relics.aspx?ID=104) - 3: [Inextinguishable](/Relics.aspx?ID=46) - 4: [Temporal Pulse](/Relics.aspx?ID=106) - 5: [Life Everlasting](/Relics.aspx?ID=48) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items_: Healing wave increases to 1d6 per relic level.
  • _Three Items_: You can use sands of the hourglass as a 30-foot line; each creature in the line must save against the effect.
  • _Four Items_: Treat the outcome of your recovery checks as one step better.
  • _Five Items_: When you use temporal pulse, each creature you slow takes 1d4 damage per relic level, and each creature you quicken regains 1d4 Hit Points per relic level. For living creatures, the damage is void, but it's vitality for undead.
name: Pharasma's Command

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 202 **Aspects** Mind and Time --- Diviners through the ages have employed a variety of tools to glimpse the future, but one who assembles five pieces of a _seer's array_ gains strong second sight. _Seer's arrays_ can be found across Golarion, from the caravans of Varisian harrowers to the scale diviners at the court of the Dragon King of Xa Hoi. A diviner who wears the array is bound to be sought after for counsel, and those who consult such farsighted individuals do well to heed any advice given. --- **Set Items**: [Pendant of the Occult](/Equipment.aspx?ID=450) (level 3), [Staff of the Unblinking Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3048) (level 6), [Clarity Goggles (Lesser)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2314) (level 8), [Crystal Ball (Clear Quartz)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3019) (level 14), [Amulet of the Third Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2132) (level 17) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Recalculate](/Relics.aspx?ID=50) - 2: [Borrow Time](/Relics.aspx?ID=103) - 3: [Time Jump](/Relics.aspx?ID=107) - 4: [Precognition](/Relics.aspx?ID=105) - 5: [Time Reversal](/Relics.aspx?ID=108) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items_: The circumstance bonus from recalculate increases by 1.
  • _Three Items_: Creatures become temporarily immune to the relic's borrow time gift for 1 hour.
  • _Four Items_: You can use the relic's time jump once per hour. For each use after the first in a given day, you must attempt a flat check with a DC of 9 + the number of times you've used the gift that day. If you fail, you can still complete the time jump, but after that the gift is no longer available until you next complete your daily preparations. You can roll the flat check before using the gift and decide not to do so after seeing the roll. However, any failure applies to the next use of the gift that day, making your next use your last that day.
  • _Five Items_: You can use the relic's precognition once per hour. Each use after the first requires a flat check like for time jump at four items, but this flat check affects your use of precognition.
name: Seer's Array

You don’t need to change your size to gain the benefits, other than reach, of a higher-level battle form. Also, your unarmed attacks from this form have the _holy_ weapon property rune.

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 202 **Aspects** Death and Shadow --- Several notable assassins, thieves, and others who work from the shadows have used an _infiltration garb_ over the centuries. The nature of these professions means that such sets are often separated from their audacious users. Due to their association with subterfuge and assassins, sets of _infiltration garbs_ have grim reputations. However, not all wielders use their powers maliciously. Notably, the faithful of [Kelinahat](/Deities.aspx?ID=473), empyreal lord of spies, stealth, and intelligence gathering, view these relics as sacred tools in the fight against evil. --- **Set Items**: [Hat of Disguise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=442) (level 2), [Goggles of Night](/Equipment.aspx?ID=438) (level 5), [Unmemorable Mantle (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=513) (level 9), [Boots of Bounding (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=412) (level 14), [Ring of Maniacal Devices (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=457) (level 18) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Obscure](/Relics.aspx?ID=185) - 2: [Death Gaze](/Relics.aspx?ID=142) - 3: [Dark Roads](/Relics.aspx?ID=188) - 4: [Tide of Death](/Relics.aspx?ID=146) - 5: [Grim Specter](/Relics.aspx?ID=147) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items:_ The relic set grants you a +1 status bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks you attempt in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403).
  • _Three Items:_ You can use [death gaze](/Relics.aspx?ID=142) to deal positive damage to undead.
  • _Four Items:_ You can use [dark roads](/Relics.aspx?ID=188) to teleport up to 60 feet.
  • _Five Items:_ When you use [tide of death](/Relics.aspx?ID=146), you can target undead and yourself with positive energy, restoring Hit Points to you (if positive energy heals you) and damaging undead harmed by positive energy.
name: Infiltration Garb

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 203 **Aspects** Emotion (dedication) and Life --- According to tales, some of the first [monks](/Classes.aspx?ID=60) to assemble the austere _monk’s attire_ were servants of [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=286). This deity is a symbol of devotion and self-discovery through one’s understanding of body, mind, and soul. _Monk’s attire_ resonates with Irori’s dedication, focusing on the highest gain from minimal acquisition, eschewing material objects. Legend holds Irori did so, and that some famous _monk’s attire_ sets include a scrap of Irori’s original possessions. Other monastic orders have similar traditions of minimal personal effects, with an emphasis on spiritual focus, practicality, and self-defense. --- **Set Items**: [Handwraps of Mighty Blows (+1)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441) (level 2), [+1 Cloth Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) (level 5) or [Bracers of Armor I](/Equipment.aspx?ID=416) (level 8), [Mask of Uncanny Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4858) (level 11) or [Ki-Channeling Beads](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4857) (level 11) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Overflowing Life](/Relics.aspx?ID=168) - 2: [Dominant Emotion](/Relics.aspx?ID=91) - 3: [Inextinguishable](/Relics.aspx?ID=169) - 3 (one 13th-level or higher item): [Emotion Conduit](/Relics.aspx?ID=94) - 3 (one 17th-level or higher item): [Quickening Emotion](/Relics.aspx?ID=96) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items:_ The item bonus from [overflowing life](/Relics.aspx?ID=168) equals the relic’s level.
  • _Three Items:_ Any [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect harmful to you triggers [dominant emotion](/Relics.aspx?ID=91), which can counteract such effects.
  • _Three Items_ (one 13th-level or higher item): Treat the outcome of your [recovery checks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2326) as one step better.
  • _Three Items_ (one 17th-level or higher item): If dominant emotion or [emotion conduit](/Relics.aspx?ID=94) [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) an effect, the target regains 5d10+5 Hit Points and gains a +2 status bonus to saving throws against mental effects for 10 minutes.
name: Monk's Attire

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 203 **Aspects** Life and Time --- [Pharasma’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) devoted followers vow to seek out and destroy undead wherever they can be found, a task made more urgent with the rise of Tar-Baphon. Though the Whispering Tyrant was prevented from taking over Golarion, his forces are still massing. [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) unaffiliated with the lich represent an unacceptable perversion of the cycle of life and death to Pharasmins, and permitting such creatures to exist is anathema to her. To aid her faithful in their sacred duty to send the dead where they belong, Pharasmin priests developed this item set. While using _Pharasma’s command,_ you must abide by her edicts and anathema or else the relic ceases to function for you until you atone. This set doesn’t function for undead beings. --- **Set Items**: [Necklace of Knives](/Equipment.aspx?ID=572) (level 2), [Holy Prayer Beads](/Equipment.aspx?ID=256) (level 5), [Wand of Overflowing Life (3rd-Level Spell)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=756) (level 9), [Cassock of Devotion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=420) (level 11) or [Cloister Robe (Greater)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2321) (level 15), [Staff of Final Rest (Major)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1285) (level 16) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Healing Wave](/Relics.aspx?ID=167) - 2: [Sands of the Hourglass](/Relics.aspx?ID=104) - 3: [Inextinguishable](/Relics.aspx?ID=169) - 4: [Temporal Pulse](/Relics.aspx?ID=106) - 5: [Life Everlasting](/Relics.aspx?ID=171) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items:_ [Healing wave](/Relics.aspx?ID=167) increases to 1d6 per relic level.
  • _Three Items:_ You can use [sands of the hourglass](/Relics.aspx?ID=104) as a 30‑foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388); each creature in the line must save against the effect.
  • _Four Items:_ Treat the outcome of your [recovery checks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2326) as one step better.
  • _Five Items:_ When you use [temporal pulse](/Relics.aspx?ID=106), each creature you slow takes 1d4 damage per relic level, and each creature you [quicken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) regains 1d4 Hit Points per relic level. For living creatures, the damage is negative, but it’s positive for [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722).
name: Pharasma's Command

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 203 **Aspects** Mind and Time --- Diviners through the ages have employed a variety of tools to glimpse the future, but one who assembles five pieces of a _seer’s array_ gains strong second sight. _Seer’s arrays_ can be found across Golarion, from the caravans of Varisian harrowers to the scale diviners at the court of the Dragon King of Xa Hoi. A diviner who wears the array is bound to be sought after for counsel, and those who consult such farsighted individuals do well to heed any advice given. --- **Set Items**: [Pendant of the Occult](/Equipment.aspx?ID=450) (level 3), [Staff of Divination](/Equipment.aspx?ID=353) (level 6), [Brooch of Inspiration](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1434) (level 8), [Crystal Ball (Clear Quartz)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3019) (level 14), [Robe of Eyes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=463) (level 17) --- **Gifts**: The set grants the following gifts. - 1: [Recalculate](/Relics.aspx?ID=173) - 2: [Borrow Time](/Relics.aspx?ID=103) - 3: [Time Jump](/Relics.aspx?ID=107) - 4: [Precognition](/Relics.aspx?ID=105) - 5: [Time Reversal](/Relics.aspx?ID=108) --- **Collective Features**: The set grants the following features.
  • _Two Items:_ The circumstance bonus from [recalculate](/Relics.aspx?ID=173) increases by 1.
  • _Three Items:_ Creatures become temporarily immune to the relic’s [borrow time](/Relics.aspx?ID=103) gift for 1 hour.
  • _Four Items:_ You can use the relic’s [time jump](/Relics.aspx?ID=107) once per hour. For each use after the first in a given day, you must attempt a [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) with a DC of 9 + the number of times you’ve used the gift that day. If you fail, you can still complete the time jump, but after that the gift is no longer available until you next complete your daily preparations. You can roll the flat check before using the gift and decide not to do so after seeing the roll. However, any failure applies to the next use of the gift that day, making your next use your last that day.
  • _Five Items:_ You can use the relic’s [precognition](/Relics.aspx?ID=105) once per hour. Each use after the first requires a flat check like for time jump at four items, but this flat check affects your use of precognition.
name: Seer's Array

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 106 You’re honorable, forthright, and committed to pushing back the forces of cruelty. You gain the Retributive Strike champion’s reaction and the [_lay on hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=395) devotion spell. After the tenets of good, add these tenets:
  • You must act with honor, never taking advantage of others, lying, or cheating.
  • You must respect the lawful authority of legitimate leadership wherever you go, and follow its laws.
Champion's Reaction Divine Smite You surround evil targets in a punishing halo. If you hit with your Retributive Strike, the target takes [persistent good damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your Charisma modifier. Exalt When you use Retributive Strike, each ally within 15 feet of you with the target in their melee reach can spend a reaction to Strike the target with a –5 penalty.
name: Paladin

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 106 You’re full of kindness and forgiveness. You gain the Glimpse of Redemption champion’s reaction and the [_lay on hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=395) devotion spell. After the tenets of good, add these:
  • You must first try to redeem those who commit evil acts, rather than killing them or meting out punishment. If they then continue on a wicked path, you might need to take more extreme measures.
  • You must show compassion for others, regardless of their authority or station.
Champion's Reaction Divine Smite A guilty conscience assails foes who spurn your Glimpse of Redemption. A foe that responds to your Glimpse of Redemption by dealing damage takes [persistent good damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your Charisma modifier. Exalt You protect multiple allies. You can apply the resistance granted by Glimpse of Redemption to yourself and all allies within 15 feet of you, including the triggering ally, except the resistance is reduced by 2 for all.
name: Redeemer

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 107 You’re committed to defending the freedom of others. You gain the Liberating Step champion’s reaction and the [_lay on hands_](/Spells.aspx?ID=395) devotion spell. After the tenets of good, add these tenets:
  • You must respect the choices others make over their own lives, and you can’t force someone to act in a particular way or threaten them if they don’t.
  • You must demand and fight for others’ freedom to make their own decisions. You may never engage in or countenance slavery or tyranny.
Champion's Reaction Divine Smite You punish those who ensnare your allies in bondage. If the triggering enemy was using any effects to make your ally [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), or [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) when you used Liberating Step, that enemy takes [persistent good damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your Charisma modifier. Exalt You can help your whole group get into position. When you use Liberating Step, if your ally doesn’t attempt to break free of an effect, you and all allies within 15 feet can Step, in addition to the triggering ally.
name: Liberator

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 116 Might makes right, and you subjugate the weak to keep them in their proper place. You gain the Iron Command champion's reaction and the [_touch of corruption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=819) devotion spell. After the tenets of evil, add these tenets.
  • Let no one who is lesser than you wield power over you or lead you.
  • Mercilessly enforce established hierarchies of masters and servants. Topple or seize control of illegitimate hierarchies, such as democratic governments or the arrogant echelons of the celestial planes, and fill power vacuums by taking that power for yourself.
  • Bind the weak to serve you. This tenet doesn't require you to spare foes' lives if you think they would be disloyal if pressed into servitude, nor does it require you to keep more servants than you find practical or useful.
Champion's Reaction Divine Smite The regrets of disobedience linger. If an enemy refuses your Iron Command, it takes [persistent evil damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your Charisma modifier. Exalt When you use Iron Command, each enemy within 15 feet of you other than the triggering creature must also either drop [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) or take mental damage (the enemy chooses). These creatures take only minimum damage (typically 3 damage at 11th level, 4 at 12th, 5 at 16th, and 6 at 19th), and the effects they take can't be adjusted by anything that changes your Iron Command. For instance, the [Iron Repercussions](/Feats.aspx?ID=1665) feat couldn't turn the damage into [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) for creatures other than the triggering creature.
name: Tyrant

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 116 You're dishonorable, dishonest, and committed to breaking the false hopes of kindness. You gain the Destructive Vengeance champion's reaction and the [_touch of corruption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=819) devotion spell. After the tenets of evil, add these tenets.
  • You must not bind yourself with any law or oath beyond that of your code (though you can still select a champion feat with the oath trait). Act dishonorably, take advantage of others, lie, cheat, and steal to get what you want.
  • You must destroy that which offends you and that which stands in your way, including—and perhaps especially—the forces of good and law that oppose you. This tenet doesn't force you to take action immediately if it could mean your destruction, nor does it require you to destroy something that might be useful if corrupted toward your ends.
Champion's Reaction Divine Smite An enemy damaged by the initial damage from your Destructive Vengeance also takes [persistent evil damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your Charisma modifier. This applies only to the damage the reaction itself deals to the triggering creature, not the damage you deal with subsequent Strikes. Exalt When you use Destructive Vengeance, each enemy within 15 feet of you other than the triggering creature takes half the damage you deal to the triggering enemy, of the same damage type you chose.
name: Antipaladin

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Exalted Reaction</title>
In addition to the enemy affected by Flash of Grandeur, each other enemy in your champion's aura is affected by the _revealing light_ spell for 1 round.

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 92

You will see all people free from bondage and prohibitions.

**Edicts**
oppose slavery and tyranny, fight for others’ freedom to make their own decisions, respect choices others make for their own lives

**Anathema**
force or threaten someone to act a certain way, engage in slavery or tyranny

<title level="2">Champion's Reaction</title>
<document level="3" id="action-2808" />

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Relentless Reaction</title>
You punish those who ensnare your allies. If the triggering enemy was using any effects to make your ally grabbed, restrained, immobilized, or paralyzed when you used Liberating Step, that enemy takes [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your Charisma modifier.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Exalted Reaction</title>
In addition to the ally affected by Liberating Step, you and all other allies in your champion’s aura can Step as a free action. You and your allies gain the benefit even if the ally who benefited from Liberating Step can’t move.
name: Liberation

<title level="1" right="Champion Cause " pfs="Standard">[Obedience](/Causes.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 93

Society is ordered with good reason, and people should conduct themselves as appropriate for their proper place.

**Edicts**
enforce proper hierarchies, topple illegitimate hierarchies, lead when you’re the most suited to the task

**Anathema**
let one who is lesser than you wield power over you or lead you

<title level="2">Champion's Reaction</title>
<document level="3" id="action-2809" />

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Relentless Reaction</title>
If the enemy refuses, it takes [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your Charisma modifier.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Exalted Reaction</title>
Each enemy in your champion's aura other than the triggering creature must also either drop [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) or take mental damage (each enemy chooses). These creatures take only minimum damage (typically 3 damage at 11th level, 4 at 12th, 5 at 16th, and 6 at 19th), and the effects they take can't be adjusted by anything that changes your Iron Command. For instance, the Iron Repercussions feat couldn't turn the damage into persistent mental damage for creatures other than the triggering creature.
name: Obedience

<title level="1" right="Champion Cause " pfs="Standard">[Redemption](/Causes.aspx?ID=14)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 93

Yearning for all to live in harmony, you make every attempt to redeem those others might slay or dismiss.

**Edicts**
try to redeem those who commit wicked deeds, show compassion to others regardless of their authority or station

**Anathema**
kill a sapient enemy without first offering a chance at redemption

<title level="2">Champion's Reaction</title>
<document level="3" id="action-2810" />

<title level="2" right="Level 9">Relentless Reaction</title>
If the enemy refuses, it takes [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your Charisma modifier.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Exalted Reaction</title>
If the enemy refuses, you can choose to grant the resistance to yourself and all allies in your champion’s aura, including the triggering ally. If you do, the resistance is reduced by 2 for all.
name: Redemption

You have a strong connection to beasts, and you are allied with a beast companion. You are trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3). You also gain the [Animal Companion](/Feats.aspx?ID=311) druid feat. You gain the [_heal animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=474) order spell. <traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 131

You revere plants and the bounty of nature, acting as both a gardener and warden for the wilderness, teaching sustainable techniques to communities, and helping areas regrow after disasters or negligent humanoid expansion. You are trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6). You also gain the [Leshy Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=312) druid feat. You gain the [_goodberry_](/Spells.aspx?ID=473) order spell, and you increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. Committing wanton cruelty to plants or fungi or killing plants unnecessarily is anathema to your order. (This doesn’t prevent you from defending yourself against plants or fungi or harvesting them when necessary for survival.)

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 132

You carry the fury of the storm within you, channeling it to terrifying effect and riding the winds through the sky. You are trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1). You also gain the [Storm Born](/Feats.aspx?ID=314) druid feat. You gain the [_tempest surge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=479) order spell, and you increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. Polluting the air or allowing those who cause major air pollution or climate shifts to go unpunished is anathema to your order. (This doesn’t force you to take action against merely potential harm to the environment or to sacrifice yourself against an obviously superior foe.)
name: Storm

You are trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7). You also gain the [Wild Shape](/Feats.aspx?ID=316) druid feat. You gain the [_wild morph_](/Spells.aspx?ID=480) order spell. Becoming fully domesticated by the temptations of civilization is anathema to your order. (This doesn’t prevent you from buying and using processed goods or staying in a city for an adventure, but you can never come to rely on these conveniences or truly call such a place your permanent home.)

<title level="1" right="Druidic Order " pfs="Limited">[Cultivation](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=12)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 72

You believe that nature is best tended, pruned, and maintained. Nature is inherently random and chaotic out of necessity of survival—the flower spreads its seeds far and wide, hoping that some might sprout, but you believe that plants truly thrive in orderly environments. Thus, you lend a hand, decluttering that which is stagnant, planting in ideal locations, replanting that which grows in suboptimal locations, mulching the soil, and performing other maintenance. While many druids might consider your approach “meddling” or “unnatural,” you know that the work you do is vital for the overall health of the environment you tend.

**Special:** The cultivation order is a variant of the [leaf](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=9) order. If you have the cultivation order, you count as a member of the leaf order, and you qualify for leaf order feats.
name: Cultivation

**Special:** The spore order is a variant of the [leaf](/DruidicOrders.aspx?ID=9) order. If you have the spore order, you count as a member of the leaf order, and you qualify for leaf order feats. Your familiar must be a fungus leshy.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Angel](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Angel" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=8" />
<trait label="Celestial" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=23" />
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 59

Your eidolon is a celestial messenger, a member of the angelic host with a unique link to you, allowing them to carry a special message to the mortal world at your side. Most angel eidolons are roughly humanoid in form, with feathered wings, glowing eyes, halos, or similar angelic features. However, some take the form of smaller angelic servitors like the winged helmet [cassisian angel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=13) instead. The two of you are destined for an important role in the plans of the celestial realms. Though a true angel, your angel eidolon's link to you as a mortal prevents them from casting the [angelic messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22) ritual, even if they somehow learn it.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

**Alignment** must be good, usually neutral good

**Home Plane** [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=20) (if NG), [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=16) (if CG), or [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=17) (if LG)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium or Small

**Suggested Attacks** fist (bludgeoning), wing (bludgeoning), unarmed attacks shaped like a weapon

**Angelic Avenger**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 8
- Wis 12
- Cha 10
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Angelic Emissary**
- Str 12
- Dex 18
- Con 12
- Int 10
- Wis 12
- Cha 14
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Senses** [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)

**Language** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Hallowed Strikes
- **Symbiosis** Traveler's Aura
- **Transcendence** Angelic Mercy

</column>

<title level="2">Hallowed Strikes</title>

Your eidolon's attacks are hallowed by the celestial realms. Your eidolon's [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strikes gain the holy trait and deal 1 extra spirit damage to unholy creatures and creatures with weakness to holy. Additionally, your eidolon can make [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks with its unarmed attacks without taking the usual –2 circumstance penalty.

<title level="2" right="7th">Traveler's Aura</title>

Your eidolon emanates a powerful aura—resembling that of an [astral deva](/Monsters.aspx?ID=16)—that protects creatures as they travel. The aura has the [abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), and [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) traits. Your eidolon and its allies within a 20-foot emanation are protected from [severe heat and cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642), and your eidolon is never [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to creatures inside the aura that are of a lower level than the eidolon.

 When you gain the transcendence class feature, this evolves to the full benefits of an astral deva; your eidolon and allies in the aura are protected from environmental damage from any plane, including severe and extreme heat and cold as well as more unusual dangers.

<title level="2" right="17th">Angelic Mercy</title>

Your eidolon's angelic power allows them to assuage a variety of ailments, just like most other powerful angels. Your eidolon can cast [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [_remove disease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=251), [_remove fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=252), and [_remove paralysis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=253) once per day each as 9th-level divine innate spells.
name: Angel

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Anger Phantom](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=2)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Ethereal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=63" />
<trait label="Phantom" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=361" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 59

Your eidolon is a lost soul, bound to the mortal world by undying anger or a bitter grudge. Most phantom eidolons are humanoids with a spectral or ectoplasmic appearance, though some take far stranger forms. Your link with your eidolon prevents it from succumbing to corruption and undeath. Together, you will need to decide whether to work with your eidolon to control its anger, or channel its wrath into power.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Home Plane** [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium or Small

**Suggested Attacks** fist (bludgeoning), tendril (bludgeoning), unarmed attacks shaped like a weapon

**Wrathful Berserker**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 8
- Wis 10
- Cha 12
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Enraged Assassin**
- Str 14
- Dex 18
- Con 16
- Int 10
- Wis 8
- Cha 12
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Language** one common mortal language the eidolon spoke in life

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Furious Strike
- **Symbiosis** Seething Frenzy
- **Transcendence** Anger Aura

</column>

<title level="2">Furious Strike</title>

Your eidolon can infuse an attack with unmitigated rage. It gains the Furious Strike activity.

<document level="3" id="action-760"/>

<title level="2" right="7th">Seething Frenzy</title>

Your eidolon can stoke itself into a frenzy. It gains the Seething Frenzy action.

<document level="3" id="action-761"/>

<title level="2" right="17th">Anger Aura</title>

Your eidolon exudes wrath in an aura around it, rousing the ire of nearby creatures and causing their defenses to crack as they seethe with rage. Your eidolon gains a 20-foot anger aura, which has the [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), and [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120) traits. The resistances of any creature in the aura, including you, your allies, your enemies, and your eidolon, are reduced by an amount equal to 3 + your eidolon's Constitution modifier. Your eidolon can hold in its rage by spending a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. This reduces the aura's effect to affect only your eidolon. It can take this action again to reinstate the full effect of its aura. Creatures in a barbarian [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3), Seething Frenzy, or similar ability that allows them to channel their anger aren't affected by the anger aura.
name: Anger Phantom

<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Beast](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=3)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 61

Your eidolon is a manifestation of the life force of nature in the form of a powerful magical beast that often has animal features, possibly even several from different species. You might have learned the way to connect with the world's life force via a specific philosophy or practice, such as the beliefs of the god callers of Sarkoris, or formed a bond on your own. Regardless, your link to your eidolon allows you both to grow in power and influence to keep your home safe from those who would despoil it.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Home Plane** [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium

**Suggested Attacks** claw (slashing), jaws (piercing), fangs (piercing), hoof (bludgeoning), horn (piercing)

**Brutal Beast**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 8
- Wis 12
- Cha 10
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Fleet Beast**
- Str 14
- Dex 18
- Con 16
- Int 8
- Wis 12
- Cha 10
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Language** [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Beast's Charge
- **Symbiosis** Primal Roar
- **Transcendence** Whirlwind Maul

</column>

<title level="2">Beast's Charge</title>

Your eidolon can make a ferocious charge or pounce on foes, allowing it to quickly engage. It gains the Beast's Charge activity.

<document level="3" id="action-762"/>

<title level="2" right="7th">Primal Roar</title>

Your eidolon can bellow a terrifying roar, screech, croak, or other overpowering sound to frighten foes. It gains the Primal Roar activity.

<document level="3" id="action-763"/>

<title level="2" right="17th">Whirlwind Maul</title>

Your eidolon thrashes violently, damaging many foes in its reach. It gains the Whirlwind Maul activity.

<document level="3" id="action-764"/>
name: Beast

<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Construct](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=4)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Astral" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=14" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=35" />
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 61

Your eidolon is a mental construct based on an astral thoughtform and given physical presence and life by its connection to you, its shape limited only by your imagination. Your eidolon's appearance and physical form vary based on your shared vision for its construction, from clockworks to stuffed dolls and everything between, and it's not uncommon for that appearance to change greatly as your construct gains evolutions. Because it arises from an astral entity, your construct is no mindless servitor, but a fully thinking being with its own ideas, goals, and even emotions. These entities are extremely diverse; while many construct eidolons come from a powerful symbiotic connection with astral denizens, just as many arise from the forgotten memories of ancient empires and craftworks drifting across the Silver Sea of the [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8).

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)

**Home Plane** [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium

**Suggested Attacks** fist (bludgeoning)

**Warrior Construct**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 12
- Wis 10
- Cha 8
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Scout Construct**
- Str 14
- Dex 18
- Con 16
- Int 12
- Wis 10
- Cha 8
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Language** one common mortal language

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Construct Heart
- **Symbiosis** Reconfigured Evolution
- **Transcendence** Ultimate Reconfiguration

</column>

<title level="2">Construct Heart</title>

Your construct eidolon has a link directly to your life force, which renders it a living creature and therefore susceptible to many ailments that bother only the living, though it does possess some resistances to these effects. It doesn't have a construct's normal immunities,  but does gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effects, as well as effects causing the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) or [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) conditions. Additionally, its astral essence bleeds off slowly, and it only needs to succeed at a DC 10 flat check to remove [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (or DC 5 after receiving particularly effective aid).

<title level="2" right="7th">Reconfigured Evolution</title>

Your construct eidolon is particularly easy to reconfigure, gaining an additional evolution that suits both of your whims. Choose one additional evolution feat of 6th level or lower. Your eidolon gains that feat. You can [Retrain](/Actions.aspx?ID=522) this feat for any other evolution feat of 6th level or lower with only a single day of downtime if you succeed at a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check, with a [standard DC of your eidolon's level](/Rules.aspx?ID=554). If you fail, you can try again on a later day. Many summoners choose [Eidolon's Wrath](/Feats.aspx?ID=2911) for this ability.

<title level="2" right="17th">Ultimate Reconfiguration</title>

Your construct eidolon becomes even more customizable. Choose an evolution feat of 16th level or lower. Your eidolon gains that feat. You can spend downtime to adjust it using [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), just like the feat from reconfigured evolution. Many summoners select [Ever-Vigilant Senses](/Feats.aspx?ID=2937) for this ability.
name: Construct

<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Restricted">[Demon](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=5)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=42" />
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 62

Your eidolon is a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), born of mortal sin congealed amid the chaos and evil of the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13), using its link to you to spread chaos at your side. Choose an associated sin for your demon. Demon eidolons have appearances as varied as the infinite Abyss. While demons are inherently untrustworthy, your demon eidolon has reached an accord with you and generally keeps it, though that doesn't mean your eidolon isn't actively working to bring your life deeper into its associated sin. Though a true demon, your demon eidolon's link to you as a mortal prevents them from casting the [_abyssal pact_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21) ritual, even if they somehow learn it.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

**Alignment** must be chaotic evil

**Home Plane** [The Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium or Small

**Suggested Attacks** claw (slashing), jaws (piercing), horn (piercing), tail (bludgeoning), tentacle (bludgeoning), wing (bludgeoning)

**Wrecker Demon**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 10
- Wis 8
- Cha 12
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Tempter Demon**
- Str 12
- Dex 18
- Con 12
- Int 10
- Wis 10
- Cha 16
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Language** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Demonic Strikes
- **Symbiosis** Visions of Sin
- **Transcendence** Blasphemous Decree

</column>

<title level="2">Demonic Strikes</title>

Your eidolon's attacks are corrupted by the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) and imbued with pure evil and shifting chaos. Your eidolon's [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strikes gain the unholy trait and deal 1 extra spirit damage to holy creatures and creatures with weakness to unholy. Additionally, choose one of your eidolon's unarmed attacks that deals physical damage; it gains your choice of [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724), versatile P, or versatile S.

<title level="2" right="7th">Visions of Sin</title>

Your demon is born of sin and can project the sin it is formed from into the minds of others. It gains the Visions of Sin activity.

<document level="3" id="action-765"/>

<title level="2" right="17th">Blasphemous Decree</title>

Your demon utters indescribable words writhing with its associated sin. It can cast an evil [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=82) once per day as a 9th-level spell. You are unaffected by this decree, even if you aren't evil. At 19th level, this becomes a 10th-level _divine decree_ instead.
name: Demon

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Devotion Phantom](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Ethereal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=63" />
<trait label="Phantom" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=361" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 63

Your eidolon is a lost soul, unable to escape the mortal world due to a strong sense of duty, an undying devotion, or a need to complete an important task. Most phantom eidolons are [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) with a spectral or ectoplasmic appearance, though some take far stranger forms. Your link with your eidolon prevents them from succumbing to corruption and undeath, and together, you will grow in strength and fulfill your phantom's devotion.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Home Plane** [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium or Small

**Suggested Attacks** fist (bludgeoning), tendril (bludgeoning), unarmed attacks shaped like a weapon

**Stalwart Guardian**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 10
- Wis 10
- Cha 10
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Swift Protector**
- Str 14
- Dex 18
- Con 16
- Int 10
- Wis 10
- Cha 10
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Language** one common mortal language the eidolon spoke in life

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Dutiful Retaliation
- **Symbiosis** Steadfast Devotion
- **Transcendence** Devotion Aura

</column>

<title level="2">Dutiful Retaliation</title>

Your eidolon deeply respects your assistance, and it extends its loyalty to you, attacking those who dare harm you. It gains the Dutiful Retaliation reaction.

<document level="3" id="action-766"/>

<title level="2" right="7th">Steadfast Devotion</title>

Your eidolon's dedication makes it extremely challenging for your foes to bend its mind. Your eidolon gains a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects, and if they roll a success against such an effect, they get a critical success instead. If you have the shared resolve class feature and your eidolon rolls a critical failure against a mental effect, they get a failure instead.

<title level="2" right="17th">Devotion Aura</title>

After fighting at your side for so long, your eidolon has become as devoted to protecting you and your allies as it is to its original cause. It gains a 20-foot devotion aura, which has the [abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), and [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120) traits. Whenever one of your eidolon's allies within the aura takes damage, you can reduce the damage by your eidolon's Constitution modifier. You lose a number of Hit Points equal to half the amount by which the eidolon reduced the damage, rounded down. If the damage was lower than the Constitution modifier, base the damage you take on the actual amount of  damage prevented.
name: Devotion Phantom

<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Dragon](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=7)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Astral" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=14" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=50" />
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 64

Because dragons have a strong connection to magic, their minds can often leave an echo floating in the [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8). Such an entity is extremely powerful but unable to interact with the outside world on its own. Dragon eidolons manifest in the powerful, scaled forms they had in life; most take the form of true dragons (albeit smaller), but some manifest as [drakes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=36) or other draconic beings. You have forged a connection with such a dragon eidolon and together, you seek to grow as powerful as an ancient wyrm.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)

**Traits** (Variable)** one more (see Breath Weapon below)

**Home Plane** [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium

**Suggested Attacks** claw (slashing), jaws (piercing), horn (piercing), tail (bludgeoning), wing (bludgeoning)

**Marauding Dragon**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 10
- Wis 10
- Cha 10
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Cunning Dragon**
- Str 12
- Dex 18
- Con 12
- Int 14
- Wis 10
- Cha 12
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Language** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Breath Weapon
- **Symbiosis** Draconic Frenzy
- **Transcendence** Wyrm's Breath

</column>

<title level="2">Breath Weapon</title>

Your eidolon has a powerful breath weapon, which they are able to use regularly to wreak havoc upon your foes. They gain the Breath Weapon activity. Choose a damage type from among [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), piercing, or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and choose whether the area is a 60-foot line or a 30-foot cone. Unless you chose piercing damage, both Breath Weapon and your eidolon gain the trait matching the damage type.

<document level="3" id="action-767"/>

<title level="2" right="7th">Draconic Frenzy</title>

Your eidolon can make a furious assault, potentially recovering the use of their Breath Weapon. They gain the Draconic Frenzy activity.

<document level="3" id="action-768"/>

<title level="2" right="17th">Wyrm's Breath</title>

Your eidolon can draw upon the power of wyrms to enhance the eidolon's Breath Weapon. Your eidolon gains the Wyrm's Breath free action.

<document level="3" id="action-769"/>
name: Dragon

<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Fey](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=8)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 64

Your eidolon is a [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), a capricious being of the mysterious [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10). Many fey appear similar to mortal [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) with unusual features such as pointed ears, wings, or bodies composed of natural elements, but the full variety of fey is endless, and many others appear completely inhuman. Fey from the First World never truly die, instead forming a new creature. Fey eidolons usually come about when a summoner helps stabilize a difficult reformation. This means your fey eidolon likely lived a different life just before meeting you and might remember fragments of its old memories. Together, you might have to unravel a memory from your eidolon's past life among the fey.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Home Plane** [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Small or Medium

**Suggested Attacks** fist (bludgeoning), wing (bludgeoning), attacks shaped like a weapon

**Skirmisher Fey**
- Str 14
- Dex 18
- Con 14
- Int 10
- Wis 10
- Cha 12
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Trickster Fey**
- Str 12
- Dex 18
- Con 12
- Int 12
- Wis 8
- Cha 16
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Language** [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Fey Gift Spells
- **Symbiosis** Fey Mischief
- **Transcendence** Fey Chicanery

</column>

<title level="2">Fey Gift Spells</title>

Your eidolon expands your primal magic with enchantment and illusion magic, allowing both of you to wield the power of fey charm and glamour. When you add spells to your repertoire, you can choose from the primal list as well as spells that have the [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) or [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits that appear on the [arcane spell list](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1). As usual for when you add spells of a different tradition to your spell list, you're still a primal spellcaster, so all of your spells are primal spells.

 Your eidolon gains the [Magical Understudy](/Feats.aspx?ID=2896) summoner feat, despite not meeting the prerequisite level, and it can choose fey gift cantrips in addition to primal cantrips. As usual for a feat you are granted by name, you can't retrain Magical Understudy into another feat.

<title level="2" right="7th">Fey Mischief</title>

Your eidolon's fey magic becomes more powerful and mischievous. Your eidolon gains the [Magical Adept](/Feats.aspx?ID=2920) feat, despite not meeting the prerequisite level, and can choose from fey gift spells in addition to primal spells.

<title level="2" right="17th">Fey Chicanery</title>

Ever full of tricks, your fey eidolon twists magical protections into your shared life force, granting it a contingency in its back pocket for desperate situations. During your daily preparations, your eidolon can cast a [_contingency_](/Spells.aspx?ID=49) spell. The 4th-level spell it chooses must come from the primal or fey gift list, and must either be common or be another spell you have access to.
name: Fey

<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Plant](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=9)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 65

Your eidolon is an intelligent plant, formed from the same disembodied fragments of nature's life energy that become leshys. Plant eidolons tend to be curious and adaptable, with temperaments based on the parts of mortal culture they feel affinity toward. Despite coming from the same source, plant eidolons don't always look like [leshys](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=67). Plant eidolons have forms that vary greatly and can look like almost any kind of plant creature in existence. Some even resemble plant creatures so strange they are impossible to identify.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Home Plane** [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium

**Suggested Attacks** vine (bludgeoning), branch (bludgeoning), root (bludgeoning) 

**Guardian Plant**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 8
- Wis 12
- Cha 10
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Creeping Plant**
- Str 12
- Dex 18
- Con 16
- Int 8
- Wis 14
- Cha 10
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Language** [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Tendril Strike
- **Symbiosis** Growing Vines
- **Transcendence** Field of Roots

</column>

<title level="2">Tendril Strike</title>

Your eidolon can stretch out vines and roots, attacking foes outside its reach. It gains the Tendril Strike action.

<document level="3" id="action-770"/>

<title level="2" right="7th">Growing Vines</title>

Your eidolon's vines and branches lengthen even more. All your eidolon's melee unarmed attacks gain the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) trait.

<title level="2" right="17th">Field of Roots</title>

Your eidolon learns how to send its roots underground to hinder foes. It gains the Field of Roots activity.

<document level="3" id="action-771"/>
name: Plant

<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Psychopomp](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=111" />
<trait label="Psychopomp" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=232" />
</traits>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 66

Your eidolon is a [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83), a creature whose sworn duty is to usher souls safely to the afterlife and maintain the courts of the dead. Psychopomp eidolons have a variety of appearances, but since they often traffic with mortals, their form typically includes an elaborate mask. You and your psychopomp eidolon share an important fate together, whether it relates directly to your own soul or to a mission that will somehow protect the souls of others.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

**Alignment** must be true neutral

**Home Plane** [The Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium

**Suggested Attacks** beak (piercing), claw (slashing), fist (bludgeoning), jaws (piercing), unarmed attacks shaped like a weapon

**Soul Guardian**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 10
- Wis 12
- Cha 8
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Scribe of the Dead**
- Str 12
- Dex 18
- Con 12
- Int 14
- Wis 12
- Cha 10
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Language** [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Spirit Touch
- **Symbiosis** Hidden Watcher
- **Transcendence** Spirit Taker

</column>

<title level="2">Spirit Touch</title>

Your eidolon's attacks possess a psychopomp's ability to harm the living and the dead. Your eidolon's unarmed Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures as though etched with a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deal an extra 1 negative damage to living creatures and an extra 1 positive damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160).

<title level="2" right="7th">Hidden Watcher</title>

Your eidolon can render both of you unseen. Once per hour, it can cast [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) targeting you, them, or both of you at once. If the spell targeted both of you, the spell ends for both of you if either of you uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300).

<title level="2" right="17th">Spirit Taker</title>

Your eidolon is particularly capable when it comes to handling bodiless spirits and putting [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) to their final rest. Your eidolon can see [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures inside of solid objects and can attack them with Strikes without penalty; an incorporeal creature in a solid object that takes damage from your eidolon's Strike is expelled from the object to the nearest available space, moving closer to your eidolon if possible.

 Additionally, your eidolon can target or affect a creature projecting its consciousness (such as via [_project image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=237)) or possessing another creature, even if its body is elsewhere, though your eidolon must know about the possession or projection and choose to do so.

 Finally, the spirit of an undead destroyed by your eidolon's Strikes is sent directly to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) for judgment; an undead with an ability to avoid such a fate automatically, such as a [ghost's](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51) rejuvenation, can attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against your spell DC to return via that ability as normal. In that case, it uses its Will save modifier as its counteract modifier.
name: Psychopomp

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Undead](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 36

Your eidolon is an undead spirit pulled from the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9) or [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2), embodied, and bound to your life force in an unusual, potentially antithetical way that even other summoners can't quite understand. Undead eidolons take about every imaginable shape and form, as their bodies manifest from their connection to you. Their ultimate form can be influenced by an amalgamation of the echoes and memories of their old life before becoming undead, their cause of death, their encounters in the afterlife, and portions of your own essences. Together, you and your eidolon need to explore the mysteries of life, death, and undeath to understand what your bond means for both of your futures.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

**Home Plane** [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9) or [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium or Small

**Suggested Attacks** claw (slashing), fist (bludgeoning), jaws (piercing)

**Undead Brute**
- Str 18
- Dex 14
- Con 16
- Int 8
- Wis 10
- Cha 12
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Undead Stalker**
- Str 14
- Dex 18
- Con 16
- Int 8
- Wis 12
- Cha 10
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Language** [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Negative Essence
- **Symbiosis** Drain Life
- **Transcendence** Rejuvenation

</column>

<title level="2">Negative Essence</title>

Your undead eidolon has a link directly to your life force, which grants it a twilight state between living and undead. This renders it a living creature and therefore susceptible to many ailments that bother only the living, though it does possess some resistances to these effects. 

The eidolon has [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), meaning it heals from negative energy effects that heal undead and is damaged by positive energy effects that damage undead. It doesn't have an undead's normal immunities, but it does gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effects, and non-damaging effects that target only undead. Additionally, its necrotic essence stanches its wounds easily. The flat check to remove [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the eidolon is DC 10 (or DC 5 after receiving particularly effective aid).

<title level="2" right="7th">Drain Life</title>

Your eidolon's link with you sustains it, but it still craves the life essence of the living, whether through blood or pure essence. It gains the Drain Life activity.

<document level="3" id="action-1168"/>

<title level="2" right="17th">Rejuvenation</title>

Your eidolon's unusual connection with you allows you both to rejuvenate when defeated. The first time each day your turn begins while you're [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11), you instantly regain a number of Hit Points equal to three times your level and wake up. (Increase your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition as normal.) You can immediately [Manifest your Eidolon](/Actions.aspx?ID=757) as a free action. You can then take your turn as normal.
name: Undead

language_markdown: [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) (air elemental), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) (earth elemental), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) (fire elemental), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) (metal elemental), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) (water elemental), or [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) (wood elemental)
markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 38 Your eidolon is a primal chunk of elemental matter infused with sapience, power, and identity, but unable to manifest a true form of their own without the life force you provide via your connection. Most elementals in their natural environment already have different sorts of forms, from vaguely humanoid, to animalistic, to simple masses of their component element. As your life force provides your eidolon the instincts necessary to adopt a physical form, their appearance varies based on the strength of their own self image and your prior exposure to elementals. Elemental eidolons tend to reach their unusual state— powerful but formless—as the result of large scale events or cataclysms, such as the war to seal the benevolent [Elemental Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=8) or their recent unsealing. Whether elemental eidolons possess any memories of a previous life or are a new sapience formed from left-over essence of a mighty servant of the Elemental Lords brought low varies from eidolon to eidolon. Together, you might undertake a journey to understand your eidolon's mysterious past or leave the past behind and forge a new destiny of your own. **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Traits** (Variable)** one more (see Elemental Core, below) **Home Plane** elemental plane matching their Elemental Core) --- **Size** Medium **Suggested Attacks** branch (bludgeoning), fist (bludgeoning), spike (piercing), tendril (bludgeoning), wave (bludgeoning) **Adaptable Elemental** - Str 1 - Dex 4 - Con 3 - Int 0 - Wis 1 - Cha 0 - +1 AC (+4 Dex cap) **Primordial Elemental** - Str 4 - Dex 2 - Con 3 - Int -1 - Wis 1 - Cha 0 - +2 AC (+3 Dex cap) **Skills** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) **Senses** [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Language** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) (air elemental), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) (earth elemental), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) (fire elemental), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) (metal elemental), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) (water elemental), or [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) (wood elemental) **Speed** 25 feet --- **Eidolon Abilities** - **Initial** Elemental Core - **Symbiosis** Elemental Burst - **Transcendence** Elemental Maelstrom Elemental Core Your elemental eidolon is an amalgam of elemental matter and mortal life force, which helps protect them from certain effects that are more harmful to mortals than elementals. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) effects, as well as effects causing the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) condition. Additionally, their elemental essence bleeds less easily than flesh, so the DC of any flat check they make to remove [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) is 10 (or 5 after receiving particularly effective aid). Choose for your eidolon to be an [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) elemental. Your eidolon and all their unarmed attacks gain the trait of the chosen element, and the eidolon gains additional effects based on your choice.
  • **Air**: Your eidolon is formed from elemental air and is light as a breeze. Your eidolon can [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36), and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) twice as far, and doesn't take falling damage.
  • **Earth**: Your eidolon is formed from elemental earth, and is incredibly hard to move by force. Your eidolon gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their Fortitude or Reflex DCs against attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) them. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to knock them [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). In addition, if any effect would force them to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet or more, they're moved only half the distance.
  • **Fire**: Your eidolon is formed from elemental fire and burns with embers of flame. Your eidolon gains resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) to fire and an equal amount of weakness to cold and water. Their unarmed attacks deal 1 additional fire damage.
  • **Metal**: Your eidolon is formed from elemental metal and can adapt their metallic form to battle. One of your eidolon's starting melee unarmed attacks gains the [versatile bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) trait, as your eidolon learns how to shift the metal into various weaponlike forms.
  • **Water**: Your eidolon is formed from elemental water and swims with ease. Your eidolon gains the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207) trait, allowing them to breathe in water and air and to avoid the normal –2 penalty for making bludgeoning and slashing unarmed Strikes underwater. Your eidolon's land Speed is reduced to 15 feet, and they gain a swim Speed of 25 feet. You can take the [Amphibious Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=2895) feat to increase their land Speed back up to 25 feet, as described in the feat.
  • **Wood**: Your eidolon is formed from elemental wood, and its living wooden form twists and regrows as you focus your elemental energies. You can choose to have your eidolon gain the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) trait. Whenever you [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71), your eidolon regains a number of Hit Points equal to double your level.
Elemental Burst Your eidolon can form projectile attacks from their own body. Your eidolon gains the Elemental Burst activity. Elemental Maelstrom Your eidolon can form a swirling vortex of elemental matter and move across the battlefield, damaging everything in their path. Your eidolon gains the Elemental Maelstrom activity.
name: Elemental
