markdown:
<title level="1" right="Summoner Eidolon " pfs="Standard">[Swarm](/Eidolons.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Eidolon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=386" />
<trait label="Swarm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=707" />
</traits>

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 72

Your eidolon is a collective of primal energy and nature spirits, gathered into a single fragmentary being. This collection of natural energy has neither form nor identity until bonded with a summoner. You give these spirits purpose, connecting with them and drawing them through to the physical realm. When manifesting, these nature spirits don a simple form, adopting the guise of many Tiny animals. Each of these animals is the same creature and is marked with your summoner’s sigil. The act of manifesting in this swarm form temporarily binds the spirits together into one being, making the eidolon a single entity—one mind and personality speaking with a multitude of voices, and a multitude of bodies acting in concert with a singular purpose.

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Home Plane** [the universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Size** Medium (condensed) and Large (dispersed)

**Suggested Attacks** beaks (piercing), claws (slashing), jaws (piercing), talons (slashing)

**Brazen Swarm**
- Str 4
- Dex 2
- Con 3
- Int 0
- Wis 0
- Cha 0
- +2 AC (+3 Dex cap)

**Slinking Swarm**
- Str 2
- Dex 4
- Con 3
- Int 0
- Wis 1
- Cha -1
- +1 AC (+4 Dex cap)

**Skills** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49)

**Senses** [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411)

**Language** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155)

**Speed** 25 feet

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Eidolon Abilities**

- **Initial** Swarm Form
- **Symbiosis** Sudden Shift
- **Transcendence** Sickening Swarm

</column>

<title level="2">Swarm Form</title>

Your eidolon is an intelligent swarm composed of hundreds of Tiny creatures, and it’s immune to the grabbed, prone, and restrained conditions. It can fit through tight spaces as though it were a Tiny creature. It has weakness to area damage equal to its level. Your eidolon has two forms: condensed and dispersed. When you Manifest Eidolon, it manifests condensed. It can switch between these two forms as a single action that has the concentrate trait. 

**Condensed Form:** When condensed, your eidolon draws all its component bodies into a single, condensed mound that functions as a Medium creature. It has a reach of 5 feet, can make Strikes, and can’t occupy the same space as other creatures. While condensed, it can use the Haunting Visage action.

**Dispersed Form:** When dispersed, your eidolon scatters its component bodies across a wider area, increasing its size by one category (to Large for most swarm eidolons). It has a reach of 0 feet, can’t make Strikes, and can occupy the same space as other creatures. While dispersed, it can use the Swarming Assault activity.

<document level="2" id="action-3306" />

<document level="2" id="action-3307" />

<title level="2" right="7th">Sudden Shift</title>

Once per round, your eidolon can switch forms between condensed and dispersed as a free action, rather than a single action. In addition, your eidolon gains the Redistribute reaction.

<document level="3" id="action-3308"/>

<title level="2" right="17th">Sickening Swarm</title>

Your eidolon is particularly good at distracting and hindering its foes while dispersed. While dispersed, spaces your eidolon occupies are difficult terrain for other creatures. In addition, your eidolon gains the Sickening Assault action.

<document level="3" id="action-3309"/>
name: Swarm

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Distant Grasp](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 16

Motion characterizes the physical—a boulder falls, creatures move, the world turns. You believe the truest form of mind over matter is therefore to move things as well, wielding telekinesis as an arm that can grasp the furthest and finest of objects.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Kinetic Ram](/Spells.aspx?ID=1156)
- **2nd** [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333)
- **3rd** [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=170)
- **4th** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125)
- **5th** [Telekinetic Haul](/Spells.aspx?ID=332)
- **6th** [Poltergeist's Fury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1160)
- **7th** [Telekinetic Bombardment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1165)
- **8th** [Falling Sky](/Spells.aspx?ID=1153)
- **9th** [Implosion](/Spells.aspx?ID=162)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177) and [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Telekinetic Rend](/Spells.aspx?ID=1130)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Vector Screen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1131)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Dancing Blade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1132)

</column>

<title level="2">Mage Hand</title>

Your _mage hand_ can carry up to 1 Bulk instead of only light Bulk. If the spell is heightened to 3rd level or higher, its maximum Bulk is 2. If the spell is heightened to 7th level or higher, its maximum Bulk is 3. It also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You create a multitude of telekinetic hands that grip onto a creature and move it about. Target a creature of Medium size or smaller with the amped spell instead of an object. You attempt to Shove the target in a direction of your choice, rolling a spell attack roll against its Fortitude DC instead of an Athletics check. The creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until the spell ends. Starting the round after you Cast the Spell, the first time each round you Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to Shove the creature again.

**Amp Heightened (4th)** You can attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) the creature instead of attempting to Shove it. If you knock an item out of the creature's grasp in this way, the _mage hand_ immediately grabs it. Any effect of the _mage hand_ on the creature ends, and the spell now carries the item, just like you had picked it up with an unamped _mage hand_.

</column>

<title level="2">Telekinetic Projectile</title>

Your telekinetic projectiles can fly much further away. Increase the range of _telekinetic projectile_ to 60 feet. It also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You fling objects with even more force, driving your opponents backwards in a hail of objects. On a success, you [push](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) the target 5 feet away from you, and on a critical success, you push the target 10 feet away from you in addition to dealing double damage.

**Amp Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6 instead of by 1d6.

</column>
name: The Distant Grasp
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 16
</additional-info>

---

<summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Infinite Eye](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 18

The true strength of the mind lies in the knowledge it contains, with each new observation contributing to the totality of its experiences. To grow your experiences—and with them, your power—you devote yourself to observing as much as possible, casting your senses through space and time with clairvoyance and precognition.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345)
- **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15)
- **3rd** [Organsight](/Spells.aspx?ID=963)
- **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=40)
- **5th** [Prying Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=239)
- **6th** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)
- **7th** [True Target](/Spells.aspx?ID=346)
- **8th** [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=351)
- **9th** [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=127)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) and [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Glimpse Weakness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1133)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Omnidirectional Scan](/Spells.aspx?ID=1134)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Foresee the Path](/Spells.aspx?ID=1135)

</column>

<title level="2">Detect Magic</title>

You have the kind of fine control that other spellcasters can only dream of. When you cast _detect magic_, you're aware of any creature in the emanation affected by a spell or carrying a magic item, and you determine the main location of magic within any [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) hazard in the emanation (with the normal restrictions on detecting illusions). This typically manifests as an alteration to your vision, such as seeing a soft aura around the creature or hazard. Sensing these phenomena doesn't make it clear which spell is on a creature, nor which of its items are magical. Your _detect magic_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Your pulse of detection magic helps you gain a sense of nearby magic's strengths or weaknesses. When you detect the presence of magic on an enemy or hazard, you gain a +1 status bonus to saves against magic originating from that enemy or hazard for 3 rounds. If you find magic from multiple sources with a single casting of _detect magic_, you get the bonus against all of them.

**Amp Heightened (7th)** The status bonus to saves against magic increases to +2.

</column>

<title level="2">Guidance</title>

You have eyes everywhere, allowing you to guide people from a great distance. The range of _guidance_ increases to 120 feet. It also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You can project a flash of insight to your ally in the nick of time to save them from failure. Amped _guidance_ doesn't cause a creature to become temporarily immune to _guidance_, and a creature can be targeted by amped _guidance_ even if it is temporarily immune to _guidance_.

 You can cast an amped _guidance_ spell as a reaction triggered when your ally fails or critically fails an attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check, and the bonus from _guidance_ would change the failure to a success or the critical failure to a normal failure. The bonus from _guidance_ applies retroactively to their check.

**Amp Heightened (6th)** The bonus from the spell increases to +2.

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 19 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Per design clarification, change the third sentence of the psychic’s Conservation of Energy ability to read “The first time in an encounter that you cast a granted spell from your conscious mind or a standard psi cantrip[...]” The intent is for Conservation of Energy to apply to produce flame and ray of frost, but not the unique cantrips, which are designed to function as written._ At the heart of all things is energy. Though it may change its form or resting place, it is eternal, fundamental, the purest of building blocks. Your powers let you shift energy—either concentrating it in a single point to explosive end or freezing objects by plundering it away— in an endless oscillation of temperature. **Granted Spells** - **1st** [Burning Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=30) - **2nd** [Heat Metal](/Spells.aspx?ID=694) - **3rd** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) - **4th** [Fire Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=118) - **5th** [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47) - **6th** [Flame Vortex](/Spells.aspx?ID=911) - **7th** [Fiery Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=115) - **8th** [Polar Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=224) - **9th** [Meteor Swarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=191) **Standard Psi Cantrips** [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539) and [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565) **Surface Psi Cantrip** [Thermal Stasis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1136) **Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Entropic Wheel](/Spells.aspx?ID=1137) **Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Redistribute Potential](/Spells.aspx?ID=1138) Conservation of Energy Energy can't be created or destroyed, only transferred or changed. Whenever you use your magic to add or remove energy, you must then balance it with the opposing force. The first time in an encounter that you cast a granted spell or a standard psi cantrip from your conscious mind, decide whether you're adding energy or removing it. Once you add energy, you must remove energy the next time you cast one of these spells. When you [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71), you restore yourself to a neutral state, allowing you to once again freely choose whether you add or remove energy on your next spell. **Adding Energy:** The ability gains the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) trait, any damage it deals is fire damage, and any resistance it grants is to cold damage. It loses any traits matching damage types it no longer deals.
**Removing Energy**: The ability gains the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) trait, any damage it deals is cold damage, and any resistance it grants is to fire damage. It loses any traits matching damage types it no longer deals. Fiery body grants ray of frost instead of produce flame when cast this way.
**Mindshift**: When you use an action that has the [mindshift](/Traits.aspx?ID=428) trait, you can choose to add or remove energy to it instead of making it mental. Alter it as normal for adding or removing energy and change any save it requires to a Reflex save. Frostbite You can freeze people from even farther away. The range of your frostbite increases to 120 feet. Your _frostbite_ also gains the following amp. **Amp** You drain thermal energy at a distance, using what you plunder to replenish yourself. The orb deals 3d4 cold damage. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to half the damage the target takes (after applying resistances and the like). You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute. **Amp Heightened (+1)** The initial damage increases by 2d4 instead of 1d4. The weakness on a critical failure increases by 1. Ignition You can drastically increase the heat against targets at a distance. When using _ignition_ as a ranged attack, increase the range to 60 feet. When using ignition as a melee attack, your reach increases by 5 feet. Your ignition also gains the following amp. **Amp** You project pure heat that causes a target to combust. The initial damage changes to 1d10 fire damage plus 1 fire splash damage. When using amped _ignition_ as a melee attack, increase the damage dice of the initial damage from d10s to d12s. You are not harmed by splash damage from amped _ignition_. **Amp Heightened (+1)** Instead of using _ignition’s_ normal heightened entry, the initial damage increases by 1d10 (1d12 for melee) and the splash damage increases by 1. The persistent fire damage on a critical hit increases by 1d4.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Silent Whisper](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 20

Every mind murmurs constantly, and the static from those nearby reverberates in your head like wind through leaves. What better focus for your magic then, than the very thoughts of those around you? Your versatile telepathic abilities let you soothe and communicate with your allies or control your enemies.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=195)
- **2nd** [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341)
- **3rd** [Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=149)
- **4th** [Telepathy](/Spells.aspx?ID=337)
- **5th** [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=327)
- **5th** [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272)
- **7th** [Visions of Danger](/Spells.aspx?ID=358)
- **8th** [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347)
- **9th** [Telepathic Demand](/Spells.aspx?ID=336)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61) and [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Forbidden Thought](/Spells.aspx?ID=1139)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Shatter Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1140)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Contagious Idea](/Spells.aspx?ID=1141)

</column>

<title level="2">Daze</title>

You can _daze_ from a great distance. The range increases to 120 feet. Your _daze_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Your spell cracks the target's mental defenses, leaving it susceptible to further psychic attack. The spell's damage changes to 1d10. If the target fails its Will save, until the end of its next turn, it gains weakness 1 to mental damage and takes a –1 status penalty to Will saves. On a critical failure, the weakness is 3 (in addition to the target being stunned 1). The weakness applies before _daze_ deals damage.

**Amp Heightened (+2)** The spell's damage increases by 2d10, and the weakness on a failure or critical failure increases by 1.

</column>

<title level="2">Message</title>

Your _message_ is not so easily bound by obstacles. The spell can travel up to 120 feet to reach the target, bending around walls and obstacles; this means you don't need a straight line of effect or line of sight to cast _message_ as long as you know the target's space and there is an unblocked path of 120 feet or less that can reach them. It also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Your telepathic message allows you to coordinate your actions while you pass on information. The target of the message can immediately spend its reaction to Step or Stride.

**Amp Heightened (4th)** The target of the message can choose to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), Strike, or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) with its reaction instead.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Tangible Dream](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 21

You pull colors and shapes from the depth of your mind, projecting impossible creations into the world as tapestries of astral thread or sculptures of force and light.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Color Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=44)
- **2nd** [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197)
- **3rd** [Sea of Thought](/Spells.aspx?ID=1162)
- **4th** [Resilient Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=255)
- **5th** [Etheric Shards](/Spells.aspx?ID=1152)
- **6th** [Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24)
- **7th** [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237)
- **8th** [Scintillating Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=267)
- **9th** [Prismatic Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=232)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528) and [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Imaginary Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1142)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Astral Rain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1143)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Hologram Cage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1144)

</column>

<title level="2">Figment</title>

Your minor illusions shift and dance. The range of _figment_ increases to 60 feet. When you Sustain the spell, you can move the apparent sound or vision up to 15 feet. You can then attempt to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=45) as usual. Those creatures who disbelieved the illusion aren't affected by this diversion. Your figment also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp**  When you amp the spell, you can create a particularly distracting illusion as part of its normal effects. Choose an unoccupied square within the spell's range. The illusion in that square provides flanking for a single melee attack made before the beginning of your next turn. If you Sustain the spell, the details of the illusion change and shift to keep your enemies unsettled; the flanking illusion's duration extends until the beginning of your next turn and you can move it to any unoccupied square in the spell's range. The flanking illusion can't provide its benefit against any creature who has disbelieved the _figment_.

</column>

<title level="2">Shield</title>

Your _shield_ spell can be deployed on behalf of others. When you cast _shield_, you can choose a target ally within 30 feet to gain the benefits of the spell instead of you. The option to [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) with the spell is up to you and uses your reaction, not the target's. Your _shield_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You create a layered barrier of three shields to improve your defenses. The duration increases to sustained up to 1 minute. The shield still lowers at the start of each of your turns, but you can raise it again when you Sustain the Spell.

 When you Shield Block with this spell, one of the three layers breaks and you cease to gain the bonus to AC, but the spell doesn't end. You can keep using the spell until the final layer breaks; once it does, the spell ends and you can't cast _shield_ or amped _shield_ again for 10 minutes. You can choose to break additional layers when you Shield Block with this spell to improve your defenses. If you break two layers instead of one, increase the Hardness to 1-1/2 × the normal amount (7 at 1st level, 15 at 3rd level, 22 at 5th level, 30 at 7th level, and 37 at 9th level). If you break all three layers, increase the Hardness to double the normal amount.

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Unbound Step](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=6)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 23

The mind can flit from thought to thought; why too shouldn't you? You focus on motion in a higher order of spatial dimensions, not just the typical three, allowing for versatile abilities that alter positioning.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Thoughtful Gift](/Spells.aspx?ID=722)
- **2nd** [Enlarge](/Spells.aspx?ID=102)
- **3rd** [Rally Point](/Spells.aspx?ID=1161)
- **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)
- **5th** [Strange Geometry](/Spells.aspx?ID=719)
- **6th** [Collective Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=43)
- **7th** [Momentary Recovery](/Spells.aspx?ID=1157)
- **8th** [Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187)
- **9th** [Bilocation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1149)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Phase Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1159) and [Warp Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1166)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Distortion Lens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1145)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Ghostly Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=1146)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Tesseract Tunnel](/Spells.aspx?ID=1147)

</column>

<title level="2">Phase Bolt</title>

Your _phase bolt_ temporarily sends the target's cover out of phase if it hits. On a success, reduce the target's circumstance bonus to AC (if any) by 1 until the beginning of your next turn. Your _phase bolt_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Your bolt leaves phase completely, becoming invisible and intangible until it's already embedded in the target— giving the impression it simply teleported itself into place. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the attack. Additionally, the bolt ignores an amount of Hardness or resistance to piercing damage equal to half the spell's level. On a critical success, the target can't be affected by [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effects until the start of your next turn.

**Amp Heightened (+1)** The bolt's damage increases by 2d4 instead of 1d4.

</column>

<title level="2">Warp Step</title>

You warp space more compactly, granting you a +10-foot status to your Speed instead of a +5-foot status bonus when you _warp step_. The spell also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Space contracts with hardly a thought, letting you Cast the Spell as a single action.

**Amp Heightened (4th)** You twist space so completely you don't need to travel the interposing distance. You can choose to instead teleport to a space within your line of sight and line of effect with a range equal to your double your Speed (after applying the status bonus from _warp step_). This grants the spell the [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) trait.

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Distant Grasp](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=7)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 16

Motion characterizes the physical—a boulder falls, creatures move, the world turns. You believe the truest form of mind over matter is therefore to move things as well, wielding telekinesis as an arm that can grasp the furthest and finest of objects.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Kinetic Ram](/Spells.aspx?ID=2573)
- **2nd** [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717)
- **3rd** [Gravity Well](/Spells.aspx?ID=1997)
- **4th** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534)
- **5th** [Telekinetic Haul](/Spells.aspx?ID=1716)
- **6th** [Poltergeist's Fury](/Spells.aspx?ID=2577)
- **7th** [Telekinetic Bombardment](/Spells.aspx?ID=2582)
- **8th** [Falling Sky](/Spells.aspx?ID=2570)
- **9th** [Implosion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1572)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) and [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Telekinetic Rend](/Spells.aspx?ID=2547)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Vector Screen](/Spells.aspx?ID=2548)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Dancing Blade](/Spells.aspx?ID=2549)

</column>

<title level="2">Telekinetic Hand</title>

Your _telekinetic hand_ can carry up to 1 Bulk instead of only light Bulk. If the spell is heightened to 3rd rank or higher, its maximum Bulk is 2. If the spell is heightened to 7th rank or higher, its maximum Bulk is 3. It also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You create a multitude of telekinetic hands that grip onto a creature and move it about. Target a creature of Medium size or smaller with the amped spell instead of an object. You attempt to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) the target in a direction of your choice, rolling a spell attack roll against its Fortitude DC instead of an Athletics check. The creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until the spell ends. Starting the round after you Cast the Spell, the first time each round you Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to Shove the creature again.

**Amp Heightened (4th)** You can attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) the creature instead of attempting to Shove it. If you knock an item out of the creature's grasp in this way, the telekinetic hand immediately grabs it. Any effect of the _telekinetic hand_ on the creature ends, and the spell now carries the item, just like you had picked it up with an unamped _telekinetic hand_.

</column>

<title level="2">Telekinetic Projectile</title>

Your _telekinetic projectiles_ can fly much further away. Increase the range of _telekinetic projectile_ to 60 feet. It also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You fling objects with even more force, driving your opponents backwards in a hail of objects. On a success, you push the target 5 feet away from you, and on a critical success, you push the target 10 feet away from you in addition to dealing double damage.

**Amp Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6 instead of by 1d6.

</column>
name: The Distant Grasp
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 16
</additional-info>

<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Infinite Eye](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=8)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 18

The true strength of the mind lies in the knowledge it contains, with each new observation contributing to the totality of its experiences. To grow your experiences—and with them, your power—you devote yourself to observing as much as possible, casting your senses through space and time with clairvoyance and precognition.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709)
- **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445)
- **3rd** [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588)
- **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466)
- **5th** [Scouting Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661)
- **6th** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)
- **7th** [True Target](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726)
- **8th** [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734)
- **9th** [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) and [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Glimpse Weakness](/Spells.aspx?ID=2550)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Omnidirectional Scan](/Spells.aspx?ID=2551)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Foresee the Path](/Spells.aspx?ID=2552)

</column>

<title level="2">Detect Magic</title>

You have the kind of fine control over your magical senses that other spellcasters can only dream of. When you cast _detect magic_, you're aware of any creature in the emanation affected by a spell or carrying a magic item, and you determine the main location of magic within any magical hazard in the emanation (with the normal restrictions on detecting [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=629)). This typically manifests as an alteration to your vision, such as seeing a soft aura around the creature or hazard. Sensing these phenomena doesn't make it clear which spell is on a creature, nor which of its items are magical. Your _detect magic_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Your pulse of detection magic helps you gain a sense of nearby magic's strengths or weaknesses. When you detect the presence of magic on an enemy or hazard, you gain a +1 status bonus to saves against magic originating from that enemy or hazard for 3 rounds. If you find magic from multiple sources with a single casting of _detect magic_, you get the bonus against all of them.

**Amp Heightened (7th)** The status bonus to saves against magic increases to +2.

</column>

<title level="2">Guidance</title>

Your clairvoyant gaze extends further than most, allowing you to guide your allies from a greater distance. The range of _guidance_ increases to 120 feet. It also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You can project a flash of insight to your ally in the nick of time to save them from failure. Amped _guidance_ doesn't cause a creature to become temporarily immune to _guidance_, and a creature can be targeted by amped guidance even if it is temporarily immune to _guidance_.

 You can cast an amped _guidance_ spell as a reaction triggered when your ally fails or critically fails an attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check, and the bonus from guidance would change the failure to a success or the critical failure to a normal failure. The bonus from _guidance_ applies retroactively to their check.

**Amp Heightened (6th)** The bonus from the spell increases to +2.

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Oscillating Wave](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=9)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 19

Energy can't be created or destroyed, only transferred or changed—whenever you use your magic to add or remove energy, you must then balance it with the opposing force. While this allows you to invert your spells to cast a scorching _frostbite_ or frozen [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), it also means your spells alternate between fire and cold in equal measure. The first time in an encounter that you cast a granted spell or standard psi cantrip from your conscious mind, decide whether you're adding energy or removing it. Once you add energy, you must remove energy the next time you cast one of these spells, and vice versa. When you Refocus, you restore yourself to a neutral state, allowing you to once again freely choose whether you add or remove energy on your next spell.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Breathe Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457)
- **2nd** [Blazing Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450)
- **3rd** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530)
- **4th** [Ice Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=2001)
- **5th** [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559)
- **6th** [Frozen Fog](/Spells.aspx?ID=2506)
- **7th** [Volcanic Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=1746)
- **8th** [Arctic Rift](/Spells.aspx?ID=1444)
- **9th** [Falling Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539) and [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Thermal Stasis](/Spells.aspx?ID=2553)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Entropic Wheel](/Spells.aspx?ID=2554)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Redistribute Potential](/Spells.aspx?ID=2555)

</column>

<title level="2">Conservation of Energy</title>

Energy can't be created or destroyed, only transferred or changed—whenever you use your magic to add or remove energy, you must then balance it with the opposing force. While this allows you to invert your spells to cast a scorching _frostbite_ or frozen [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), it also means your spells alternate between fire and cold in equal measure. The first time in an encounter that you cast a granted spell or standard psi cantrip from your conscious mind, decide whether you're adding energy or removing it. Once you add energy, you must remove energy the next time you cast one of these spells, and vice versa. When you Refocus, you restore yourself to a neutral state, allowing you to once again freely choose whether you add or remove energy on your next spell.

  **Adding Energy:** The ability gains the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) trait and any damage it deals is fire damage. It loses any traits matching damage types it no longer deals.

 **Removing Energy:** The ability gains the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) trait and any damage it deals is cold damage. It loses any traits matching damage types it no longer deals.

 **Mindshift:** When you use an action that has the [mindshift](/Traits.aspx?ID=900) trait, you can choose to add or remove energy to it instead of making it mental. Alter it as normal for adding or removing energy and change any save it requires to a Reflex save.

<title level="2">Frostbite</title>

You can freeze people from even farther away. The range of your _frostbite_ increases to 120 feet. Your _frostbite_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You drain thermal energy at a distance, using what you plunder to replenish yourself. The orb deals 3d4 cold damage. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to half the damage the target takes (after applying resistances and the like). You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute.

**Amp Heightened (+1)** The initial damage increases by 2d4 instead of 1d4. The weakness on a critical failure increases by 1.

</column>

<title level="2">Ignition</title>

You can drastically increase the heat against targets at a distance. When using _ignition_ as a ranged attack, increase the range to 60 feet. When using _ignition_ as a melee attack, your reach increases by 5 feet. Your _ignition_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You project pure heat that causes a target to combust. The initial damage changes to 1d10 fire damage plus 1 fire splash damage. When using amped _ignition_ as a melee attack, increase the damage dice of the initial damage from d10s to d12s. You are not harmed by splash damage from amped _ignition_.

**Amp Heightened (+1)** Instead of using ignition's normal heightened entry, the initial damage increases by 1d10 (1d12 for melee) and the splash damage increases by 1. The [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on a critical hit increases by 1d4.

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Silent Whisper](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=10)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 20

Every mind murmurs constantly, and the static from those nearby reverberates in your head like wind through leaves. What better focus for your magic then, than the very thoughts of those around you? Your versatile telepathic abilities let you soothe and communicate with your allies or control your enemies.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603)
- **2nd** [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691)
- **3rd** [Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555)
- **4th** [Telepathy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719)
- **5th** [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710)
- **5th** [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665)
- **7th** [Visions of Danger](/Spells.aspx?ID=2040)
- **8th** [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1730)
- **9th** [Telepathic Demand](/Spells.aspx?ID=2036)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482) and [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Forbidden Thought](/Spells.aspx?ID=2556)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Shatter Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=2557)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Contagious Idea](/Spells.aspx?ID=2558)

</column>

<title level="2">Daze</title>

You can _daze_ from a great distance, your thoughts resounding like a siren in your foes' minds. The range increases to 120 feet. Your _daze_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Your spell cracks the target's mental defenses, leaving it susceptible to further psychic attack. The spell's damage changes to 1d10. If the target fails its Will save, until the end of its next turn, it gains weakness 1 to mental damage and takes a –1 status penalty to Will saves. On a critical failure, the weakness is 3 (in addition to the target being [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1). The weakness applies before _daze_ deals damage.

**Amp Heightened (+2)** The spell's damage increases by 2d10, and the weakness on a failure or critical failure increases by 1.

</column>

<title level="2">Message</title>

Your _message_ spell can travel up to 120 feet to reach the target, bending around walls and obstacles; this means you don't need a straight line of effect or line of sight to cast _message_ as long as you know the target's space and there is an unblocked path of 120 feet or less that can reach them. You can cast _message_ in a purely telepathic manner, causing the spell to lose the auditory trait. It also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Your telepathic message allows you to coordinate your actions while you pass on information. The target of the message can immediately spend its reaction to Step or Stride.

**Amp Heightened (4th)** The target of the message can choose to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), Strike, or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) with its reaction instead.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Tangible Dream](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=11)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 21

You pull colors and shapes from the depth of your mind, projecting impossible creations into the world as tapestries of astral thread or sculptures of force and light.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Dizzying Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500)
- **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577)
- **3rd** [Sea of Thought](/Spells.aspx?ID=2579)
- **4th** [Containment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1981)
- **5th** [Etheric Shards](/Spells.aspx?ID=2569)
- **6th** [Scintillating Safeguard](/Spells.aspx?ID=2023)
- **7th** [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=1640)
- **8th** [Confusing Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1980)
- **9th** [Resplendent Mansion](/Spells.aspx?ID=2018)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528) and [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Imaginary Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=2559)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Astral Rain](/Spells.aspx?ID=2560)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Hologram Cage](/Spells.aspx?ID=2561)

</column>

<title level="2">Figment</title>

Your minor illusions shift and dance in a way that draws the eye. The range of _figment_ increases to 60 feet. When you Sustain the spell, you can move the apparent sound or vision up to 15 feet. You can then attempt to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387) as usual. Those creatures who disbelieved the illusion aren't affected by this diversion. Your _figment_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** When you amp the spell, you can create a particularly distracting illusion as part of its normal effects. Choose an unoccupied square within the spell's range. The illusion in that square provides flanking for a single melee attack made before the beginning of your next turn. If you Sustain the spell, the details of the illusion change and shift to keep your enemies unsettled; the flanking illusion's duration extends until the beginning of your next turn and you can move it to any unoccupied square in the spell's range. The flanking illusion can't provide its benefit against any creature who has disbelieved the _figment_.

</column>

<title level="2">Shield</title>

Your _shield_ spell can be deployed on behalf of others. When you cast _shield_, you can choose a target ally within 30 feet to gain the benefits of the spell instead of you. The option to [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) with the spell is up to you and uses your reaction, not the target's. Your _shield_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** You create a layered barrier of three _shields_ to improve your defenses. The duration increases to sustained up to 1 minute. The shield still lowers at the start of each of your turns, but you can raise it again when you Sustain the Spell.

 When you Shield Block with this spell, one of the three layers breaks and you cease to gain the bonus to AC, but the spell doesn't end. You can keep using the spell until the final layer breaks; once it does, the spell ends and you can't cast shield or amped _shield_ again for 10 minutes. You can choose to break additional layers when you Shield Block with this spell to improve your defenses. If you break two layers instead of one, increase the Hardness to 1-1/2 × the normal amount (7 at 1st rank, 15 at 3rd rank, 22 at 5th rank, 30 at 7th rank, and 37 at 9th rank). If you break all three layers, increase the Hardness to double the normal amount.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Psychic Conscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[The Unbound Step](/ConsciousMinds.aspx?ID=12)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 23

The mind can flit from thought to thought; why too shouldn't you? You focus on motion in a higher order of spatial dimensions, not just the typical three, allowing for versatile abilities that alter positioning.

<column gap="tiny">

**Granted Spells**
- **1st** [Thoughtful Gift](/Spells.aspx?ID=2037)
- **2nd** [Enlarge](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514)
- **3rd** [Rally Point](/Spells.aspx?ID=2578)
- **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
- **5th** [Strange Geometry](/Spells.aspx?ID=2032)
- **6th** [Collective Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1978)
- **7th** [Momentary Recovery](/Spells.aspx?ID=2574)
- **8th** [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644)
- **9th** [Bilocation](/Spells.aspx?ID=2566)

**Standard Psi Cantrips** [Phase Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=2576) and [Warp Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=2583)

**Surface Psi Cantrip** [Distortion Lens](/Spells.aspx?ID=2562)

**Deeper Psi Cantrip (Level 6)** [Ghostly Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=2563)

**Deepest Psi Cantrip (Level 10)** [Tesseract Tunnel](/Spells.aspx?ID=2564)

</column>

<title level="2">Phase Bolt</title>

Your _phase bolt_ temporarily sends the target's cover out of phase if it hits. On a success, reduce the target's circumstance bonus to AC (if any) by 1 until the beginning of your next turn. Your _phase bolt_ also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Your bolt leaves phase completely, becoming invisible and intangible until it's already embedded in the target—giving the impression it simply teleported itself into place. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the attack. Additionally, the bolt ignores an amount of Hardness or resistance to piercing damage equal to half the spell's rank. On a critical success, the target can't be affected by [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effects until the start of your next turn.

**Amp Heightened (+1)** The bolt’s damage increases by 2d4 instead of 1d4.

</column>

<title level="2">Warp Step</title>

You warp space more compactly, granting you a +10-foot status to your Speed instead of a +5-foot status bonus when you _warp step_. The spell also gains the following amp.

<column gap="tiny">

**Amp** Space contracts with hardly a thought, letting you Cast the Spell as a single action.

**Amp Heightened (4th)** You twist space so completely you don't need to travel the interposing distance. You can choose to instead teleport to a space within your line of sight and line of effect with a range equal to your double your Speed (after applying the status bonus from warp step). This grants the spell the [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) trait.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Weather Hazard 6" pfs="">[Blizzard](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Weather" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=464" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 123

A combination of heavy snowfall and wind reduces visibility and temperature significantly.

---

**Requirements**
A blizzard can occur only during winter.

**Survival** DC 25 (expert)

**Preparation**
DC 21 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) (trained) to notice escalations in the weather or 25 DC [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) (expert) to correctly interpret the behavior of local wildlife.

**Routine** (8+ hours) For the blizzard's duration, visibility is reduced to such an extent that visual Perception checks have a –4 circumstance penalty. Windstorm conditions (page 125) apply during a blizzard, and the regional temperature drops to [severe cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642). Once a blizzard starts, an unsheltered character takes 2d6 cold damage every hour. During a blizzard, a DC 25 Survival check is enough to find or create shelter to avoid additional cold damage from exposure, but each attempt to do so takes 1 hour. Once a blizzard ends, travel through the zone is treated as difficult terrain for a number of days equal to the number of flat checks that were required to end the blizzard.
name: Blizzard
requirement_markdown: A blizzard can occur only during winter.

<title level="1" right="Weather Hazard 1" pfs="">[Cold Snap](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=2)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Weather" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=464" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 123

The temperature drops noticeably, becoming unusually cold for this time of year.

---

**Requirements**
A cold snap can occur at any time except during the summer.

**Survival** DC 16 (trained)

**Preparation**
DC 19 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) (trained) or DC 16 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) (trained) to realize there's an imminent shift in weather.

**Routine** (24+ hours) The [temperature](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) drops down to an unusually low level. During spring and autumn, the temperature shifts to mild cold. During winter, the temperature shifts to severe cold, during which unsheltered PCs take 1d6 cold damage per hour. If a [blizzard](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=1) takes place during a cold snap, the blizzard's temperature drops to extreme cold, during which unsheltered PCs take 2d6 cold damage every 10 minutes.
name: Cold Snap
requirement_markdown: A cold snap can occur at any time except during the summer.

markdown:
**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 123 Heavy rainfall upriver or deeper in the mountains causes a sudden rush of water to come cascading downstream or along an otherwise dry riverbed. --- **Requirements** The PCs are in a hex that contains hills, mountains, or a river. **Survival** DC 27 (expert) **Preparation** DC 21 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) (trained) to move to high ground before the flash flood hits. **Routine** (1+ hours) The rushing waters of the flood surge through the area with shocking speed. The PCs roll initiative with Perception or Survival checks; determine the flash flood's initiative by rolling 1d20+17. On a PC's turn, they can attempt to move out of the flood's path. This could be as simple as taking Strides to get out of the way, or this could be complicated by having to succeed at a DC 25 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to climb to safety. You can also complicate escape by having the width of the flood area be more significant, to an extent that even three Strides might not be enough to escape. On the flash flood's turn, it attempts to Sweep Away any PCs who haven't escaped its path. A character who is swept away can escape the flood by succeeding at a DC 25 Athletics check as a three-round Interact action to swim to shore or cling to an outcropping, tree, or other solid object; otherwise, the PC will continue to be Swept Away round after round. Once the flood ends, the waters of the river left behind remain deep for several more hours or, at your discretion, even days.
**Sweep Away** The flash flood sweeps away characters who haven't escaped its path. Each character is carried 200 feet downriver (they can avoid this by succeeding at a DC 30 Athletics check to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39)) and takes 6d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
name: Flash Flood
requirement_markdown: The PCs are in a hex that contains hills, mountains, or a river.

**Routine** (1+ hours) During a severe hailstorm, periodic flurries of hail measuring an inch or more in diameter buffet the area. Any character who doesn't have shelter during a severe hailstorm takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) over the course of the hour. A severe hailstorm also limits vision in the same manner as [heavy downpour](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=6).

As long as the heavy downpour lasts, visual Perception checks take a –1 circumstance penalty, causes creatures that are further than 100 feet away to be concealed, and cuts off all visibility at half a mile or more.
During a heavy downpour, other storms tend to be even more severe. The DC of skill checks made to survive, prepare, or endure the routine of a [blizzard](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=1), [severe hailstorm](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=5), [supernatural storm](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=8), [thunderstorm](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=9), or [windstorm](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=12) increase by +2 during heavy downpour. Rivers tend to rise during heavy downpours. Shallow points to cross rivers become too deep to ford and remain so for 1d4 days after the event.

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 124 With little warning, the ground itself suddenly gives way, be it a caused by a small avalanche, rockslide, mudslide, or the sudden opening of a sinkhole. --- **Survival** DC 22 (expert) **Preparation** DC 22 Perception to take a less dangerous path through the region. **Routine** A subsidence has no duration. Those who aren't prepared when it occurs attempt a DC 22 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The character avoids the unexpected subsidence entirely.
**Success** The character takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage from the subsidence.
**Failure** The character takes 8d6 bludgeoning damage from the subsidence. If the subsidence is a sinkhole, the character ends up at the bottom of a hole 1d6 × 5 feet down and can clamber out with a successful DC 22 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check. If the subsidence is a mudslide, rockslide, or avalanche, the character is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and swept 200 feet away.
**Critical Failure** The character takes 16d6 bludgeoning damage from the subsidence and is buried. Buried PCs are at risk of [suffocation](/Rules.aspx?ID=468); see [Burial](/Rules.aspx?ID=647) for details on how a buried creature can be rescued. --- **Major Subsidence** (12th) Increase DCs associated with the subsidence to 32, and bludgeoning damage increases to 8d6 for a successful save, 16d6 for a failed save, and 32d6 for a critical failure.
name: Subsidence

<title level="1" right="Weather Hazard 6" pfs="">[Supernatural Storm](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=8)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Weather" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=464" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 124

What starts out as a [heavy downpour](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=6) or a [windstorm](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=12) takes a turn for the outlandish, as something strange begins raining down from the heavens above: bolts of fire, acid rain, or even something like a rain of frogs or shards of stained glass.

---

**Survival** DC 25 (expert)

**Preparation**
DC 25 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) (trained) or DC 25 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) (trained) to find shelter in the region.

**Routine** (1+ hours) Supernatural storms are the result of the growing influence of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) over the region. When they occur, characters who aren't in shelter take 4d8+18 points of damage each hour (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The type of damage depends on the type of storm, as chosen by you. For example, acid rain would cause acid damage, while a rain of frogs would cause bludgeoning damage.

---

**Escalated Storm** (Level +1) Supernatural storms can scale upward in level as you need to continue challenging the PCs. Use the tables for [building hazards](/Rules.aspx?ID=1043) to set the escalated storm's DCs—it uses the high DCs for Survival and Prepare from [Table 2–13](/Rules.aspx?ID=1047) of that book, and the damage value is the [Simple Dmg value from Table 2–16](/Rules.aspx?ID=1049).
name: Supernatural Storm

<title level="1" right="Weather Hazard 7" pfs="">[Thunderstorm](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=9)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Weather" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=464" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 124

Dark clouds announce their arrival with distant peals of thunder and flashes of lightning.

---

**Survival** DC 27 (expert)

**Preparation**
DC 23 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) (trained) to minimize chances of being struck by lightning.

**Routine** (8+ hours) A thunderstorm functions as a [heavy downpour](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=6) during its duration, but with the added danger of being struck by lightning. If the PCs prepare for a thunderstorm, they can avoid lightning strikes and still travel by taking the [Defend](/Actions.aspx?ID=512) exploration activity (taking care to move carefully, staying low, and avoiding trees and the like rather than raising shields). A PC who travels without taking this activity or doesn't seek shelter must attempt a DC 6 flat check each hour; on a failure, that PC is struck by lightning and takes 8d8 electricity damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

---

**Severe Thunderstorm** (13th) A severe thunderstorm's Survival DC increases to 37 and its Prepare DC to 32. A PC at risk of being struck by lightning must attempt a DC 10 flat check 1d4 times each hour and is struck by lightning on each failure for 14d8 electricity damage (DC 33 basic Reflex save).
name: Thunderstorm

markdown:
**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 125 Strangely colored or ominously shaped clouds often presage the arrival of a distinctive funnel cloud's arrival in the region. --- **Requirements** The PCs are in a hex that contains no significant hills or mountains. **Survival** DC 27 (expert) **Preparation** DC 35 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) (master) to know how best to prepare for the tornado if no existing shelters are available. **Routine** (1+ hours) When a tornado touches down, the funnel cloud can wreak amazing damage. While the area affected by a tornado is relatively limited, predicting where the tornado will travel during its brief, destructive lifetime is difficult. Underground chambers can provide shelter from tornadoes, but characters who seek shelter above ground are still in danger. Each hour that a tornado persists in the area, attempt a DC 16 flat check (for each PC sheltering above ground) or a DC 6 flat check (for each PC who isn't sheltered at all). On a success, that PC is exposed to the tornado's winds or its hurled debris and must attempt a DC 32 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The character avoids the tornado and its debris.
**Success** The character takes 4d6 bludgeoning and 4d6 piercing damage from hurled debris, but they avoid further damage from that particular tornado for the remainder of the hour.
**Failure** The character takes 8d6 bludgeoning and 8d6 piercing damage from hurled debris, and they must save against the tornado's effects again on the following round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the character is also lifted into the air and hurled 120 feet in a random direction, taking an additional 60 points of bludgeoning damage from the fall. --- **Tornado Swarm** (17th) The most devastating of these storms involve multiple tornadoes touching down at once. In a tornado swarm, the Survival DC increases to 34, the Prepare DC to 43, and the save DC against the tornado's effects to 38. Additionally, each hour that the tornado swarm persists in the area, make four flat checks for each PC, potentially exposing a PC to many more tornado saving throws in an hour.
name: Tornado
requirement_markdown: The PCs are in a hex that contains no significant hills or mountains.

<title level="1" right="Weather Hazard 4" pfs="">[Wildfire](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Weather" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=464" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 125

A fire spreads through the region, be it one accidentally started by a poorly maintained campfire, deliberate arson, a lightning strike, or from a supernatural source like a creature's fiery breath or a fire spell being cast by a bandit in the region. Wildfires cannot occur during [heavy downpours](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=6) or [blizzards](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=1), and if such an event starts in the area, it brings an existing wildfire to an end.

---

**Survival** DC 22 (trained)

**Preparation**
DC 18 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) (trained) or DC 25 Perception to notice plumes of smoke or the tang of smoke in the air.

**Routine** (1+ hour) An unsheltered character must succeed at a DC 21 Reflex save or take 6d6 fire damage (12d6 fire damage on a critical failure). If the character has sheltered in a temporary shelter or a flammable building, attempt a DC 16 flat check each hour the fire persists; on a failure, the fire consumes the shelter.

---

**Severe Wildfire** (10th) A severe wildfire has a duration of 8+ hours. Unsheltered characters must succeed at a DC 29 Reflex save or take 12d6 fire damage (24d6 fire damage on a critical failure), and it's a DC 11 flat check for the temporary or flammable shelter to avoid burning. The heavy smoke in the area is thick enough to function as [fog](/Rules.aspx?ID=4).
name: Wildfire

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 84

To you, a fight is a kind of performance art, and you command your foes' attention with mesmerizing motions. You are trained in Performance and gain the [Fascinating Performance](/Feats.aspx?ID=781) skill feat. You gain panache during an encounter when the result of your [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check to [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59) exceeds the Will DC of an observing foe, even if the foe isn't [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14).

<title level="2" right="9th">Exemplary Finisher</title>
You can Step as a free action immediately after the finisher.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 161

You move carefully, feinting and creating false openings to lead your foes into inopportune attacks. You are trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38). When you [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390), the action gains the [bravado](/Traits.aspx?ID=801) trait.

<title level="2" right="9th">Exemplary Finisher</title>
The foe is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until your next turn.

<title level="1" right="Swashbuckler Style " pfs="Standard">[Gymnast](/Styles.aspx?ID=10)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 161

You reposition, maneuver, and bewilder your foes with daring feats of physical prowess. You are trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36). When you [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382), the action gains the [bravado](/Traits.aspx?ID=801) trait.

<title level="2" right="9th">Exemplary Finisher</title>
If the foe is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), or [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), you gain a circumstance bonus to the damage roll equal to double the number of weapon damage dice.
name: Gymnast

You aren’t afraid to use underhanded tactics to get the edge over your opponents. You are trained in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) and gain the [Dirty Trick](/Feats.aspx?ID=6472) general feat. When you use Dirty Trick, the action gains the [bravado](/Traits.aspx?ID=801) trait.

The foe takes a –10 circumstance penalty to its speed until the start of your next turn.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 161

You are friendly, clever, and full of humor, always knowing just what to say. Your witticisms leave foes unprepared for the skill and speed of your attacks. You are trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) and gain the [Bon Mot](/Feats.aspx?ID=6466) skill feat. When you use Bon Mot, the action gains the [bravado](/Traits.aspx?ID=801) trait.

<title level="2" right="9th">Exemplary Finisher</title>
The foe takes a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn.

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 572 Greengripe [goblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) (encounter site **SH5**) have built a mobile platform outfitted with a catapult-like flinging arm that can throw flammable debris. --- **Scouting** +13 (-2 adjustment) **Recruitment** 28 (+5 adjustment) **Consumption** 2 **AC** 23 (-2 adjusment) **Maneuver** +12 (low) **Morale** +18 (high) **HP** 6 (RT 3) **Melee** dogslicers and torches +19 ( +1 adjustment) **Ranged** burning debris +18 (5 shots) **Burning Weaponry** (unique tactic) Unlike basic siege engine armies, Greengripe Bombardiers can make melee Strikes while [engaged](/Conditions.aspx?ID=48). When a Greengripe Bombardier scores a critical success on a Strike while taking the Battle action against an army (but not against a fortification), the army struck must make a successful Maneuver check against the Greengripe Bombardiers' Maneuver DC or take one additional point of damage from the goblins' burning weaponry.
**Darkvision** (unique tactic)
**Explosive Defeat** (unique tactic) When the Greengripe Bombardiers are [defeated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=45), their alchemical siege engine explodes. Any army [engaged](/Conditions.aspx?ID=48) with the Greengripe Bombardiers must succeed at a [Maneuver](/Traits.aspx?ID=459) check against the Greengripe Bombardier's Maneuver DC or take 1 point of damage (2 points of damage on a critical failure). As long as the Greengripe Bombardiers aren't destroyed, their siege engine gear is rebuilt automatically once the army loses the defeated condition.
name: Greengripe Bombardiers

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 572 These lance-armed [boggards](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=16) from M'botuu (encounter site **HT6**) and ride giant frogs trained for warfare into battle. --- **Scouting** +19 **Recruitment** 32 (+5 adjustment) **Consumption** 2 **AC** 27 **Maneuver** +22 (high) **Morale** +18 (low, +2 adjustment) **HP** 6 (RT 3) **Melee** lances +25 (+2 adjustment) **Ranged** javelins +23 (5 shots) **Amphibious** (unique tactic) Frog Riders ignore difficult terrain caused by swamps or water and gain a +2 circumstance bonus on [Maneuver](/Traits.aspx?ID=459) checks while fighting on a battlefield that features either of these terrains.

The M'botuu Frog Riders can use the Taunt tactical action. If they know the Focused Devotion tactic, they treat critical failures made when attempting to Taunt as failures instead.
**Darkvision** (unique tactic)
**Swamp Charge** (unique tactic) If the battlefield includes swamp or water, the M'botuu Frog Riders can start the encounter [engaged](/Conditions.aspx?ID=48) with a target enemy army. If they do so, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus on melee Strikes against that army on the first turn of the encounter.
name: M'Botuu Frog Riders

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 573 This band of Nomen [centaurs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=76) (encounter site **NH1**) fight with spears and longbows. --- **Scouting** +18 (+2 adjustment) **Recruitment** 26 (+2 adjustment) **Consumption** -1 (self-sufficient) **AC** 27 **Maneuver** +14 (low, +1 adjustment) **Morale** +19 (high) **HP** 4 (RT 0) **Melee** spears +20 **Ranged** longbows +20 (5 shots) **Brave** (unique tactic) Nomen scouts are extraordinarily fearless and do not possess a Rout Threshold, and gain a +2 circumstance bonus on [Morale](/Traits.aspx?ID=460) checks made to avoid rout from other sources.
**Darkvision** (unique tactic)
**Self-Sufficient** (unique tactic) Nomen scouts are adept at providing for themselves, and never count against the kingdom's consumption. Furthermore, they can provide for other armies, and as long as they are not defeated during a Kingdom turn's Upkeep phase, they reduce the kingdom's consumption value by 1.
**Trample** (unique tactic) Nomen Scouts can attempt to trample an [engaged](/Conditions.aspx?ID=48) enemy army by attempting a +20 melee Strike against the army's AC. On a hit, they inflict 1 point of damage (2 points on a critical hit) and automatically move away from the army—they are no longer engaged with that army.
name: Nomen Scouts

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 573 Sootscale [kobolds](/Rules.aspx?ID=18) (encounter site **GB20**) fight with shortswords and crossbows, although they tend to do so warily and cautiously. --- **Scouting** +9 **Recruitment** 20 (+2 adjustment) **Consumption** 1 **AC** 20 (+1 adjustment) **Maneuver** +14 (high, +2 adjustment) **Morale** +5 (low, -1 adjustment) **HP** 4 (RT 3) **Melee** shortswords +12 **Ranged** crossbows +12 (7 shots) **Accustomed to Panic** (unique tactic) **Trigger** The Sootscale Warriors' [shaken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=56) condition increases while they are not [routed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=55). **Effect** The Sootscales take comfort in their fear and channel the panic into energy. **Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 573 These Tiger Lord barbarians (encounter area **TL3**) use rage in battle; they fight with greataxes. --- **Scouting** +23 (+1 adjustment) **Recruitment** 32 (+2 adjustment) **Consumption** 1 **AC** 32 (-1 adjustment) **Maneuver** +19 (low) **Morale** +25 (high) **HP** 6 (RT 2) **Melee** greataxes +28 (+2 adjustment) **Furious Charge** (unique tactic) **Frequency** once per war encounter; **Effect** The Tiger Lord Berserkers charge at an enemy army, taking an Advance action to attempt to engage. If the Tiger Lords manage to engage successfully, they can attempt a melee Strike against the army as a reaction.
**Reactive Rally** (unique tactic) **Requirement** The Tiger Lords are [routed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=55); **Effect** Instead of taking a Retreat action, the Tiger Lord Berserkers can attempt to Rally. **Revel in Battle** (unique tactic) **Trigger** The Tiger Lord Berserkers score a critical hit with a melee Strike. **Effect** The barbarians are infused with vigor and furious passion at their devastating blow. The army restores 1 hit point.
**Warmongers** (unique tactic) The Tiger Lord Berserkers can use the following Tactical Actions without needing to meet additional requirements: All-Out Assault, Counterattack, and Taunt.

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 573 Capable of striding across water, these [bog striders](/Monsters.aspx?ID=574) from the settlement of Tok-Nikrat (encounter area **HT12**) fight with nets and spears. --- **Scouting** +20 (+1 adjustment) **Recruitment** 32 (+5 adjustment) **Consumption** 1 **AC** 31 (+1 adjustment) **Maneuver** +22 (high) **Morale** +18 (low, +2 adjustment) **HP** 4 (RT 2) **Melee** spears +23 **Ranged** net +23 (5 shots) **Darkvision** (unique tactic)
**Hurl Nets** (unique tactic) If the Tok-Nikrat Scouts hit an army with a net Strike, the army takes no damage. Instead, it increases its [mired](/Conditions.aspx?ID=51) condition value by 1 (or by 2 on a critical success). An army that is mired as a result of hurled nets can attempt to reduce the value of this mired condition (but not that caused by other sources) by attempting a Battle action against the Tok-Nikrat Scout's Maneuver DC. On a success, the army reduces their mired condition by 1 (or by 2 on a critical success), but on a critical failure, they increase the mired condition caused by the hurled nets by 1. Tok-Nikrat Scouts can't hurl nets at a target army that is [distant](/Conditions.aspx?ID=52).
**Water Retreat** (unique tactic) If the battlefield features water terrain, Tok-Nikrat Scouts gain a +4 circumstance bonus on Maneuver checks made to Disengage.
**Water Stride** (unique tactic) The Tok-Nikrat Scouts stride over watery surfaces, and they ignore difficult terrain caused by water or swamps. They gain a +4 circumstance bonus on initiative checks in war encounters when the battlefield features either terrain.
name: Tok-Nikrat Scouts

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 108

You're a wanderer traveling from land to land with your gun and a melee weapon as company. <title level="1" right="Gunslinger Way " pfs="Standard">[Way of the Spellshot](/Ways.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 140

Prerequisites: Must be a [gunslinger](/Classes.aspx?ID=20).

You've learned the intricacies of magic and technology, blending the two together with deadly results. You combine a knowledge of arcane theories with a strong connection to your chosen weapon that allows you to manifest unique effects, though your knowledge doesn't extend as far as actual spellcasting. You can imbue energy into your weapon and conjure bullets from thin air. As you progress, you eventually learn to phase bullets through walls or even to teleport yourself along the path of your bullets.

You must select [Spellshot Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3267) from the [Spellshot](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=122) archetype as your 2nd-level class feat.

**Way Skill** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane Thesis " pfs="Standard">[Improved Familiar Attunement](/ArcaneThesis.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 205

You've long held that fine-tuning the magic that bonds wizard and familiar can improve the mystic connection, compared to the safe yet generic bond most wizards currently use. You've formed such a pact with your familiar, gaining more advantages from it than most wizards. You gain the [Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=604) wizard feat. Your familiar gains an extra ability, and it gains an additional extra ability when you reach 6th, 12th, and 18th levels. Your connection with your familiar alters your arcane bond class feature so that you store your magical energy in your familiar, rather than an item you own; you also gain the Drain Familiar free action instead of Drain Bonded Item. Drain Familiar can be used any time an ability would allow you to use Drain Bonded Item and functions identically, except that you draw magic from your familiar instead of an item.
name: Improved Familiar Attunement

<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane Thesis " pfs="Standard">[Metamagical Experimentation](/ArcaneThesis.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 205

You've realized that the practice known as metamagic is a holdover from a time long ago, when wizards had to work out their own spells and variations rather than rely on spells recorded by others and passed down over the years. This allows you efficient access to various metamagic effects.

You gain one 1st-level [metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107) wizard feat of your choice. Starting at 4th level, during your daily preparations, you can gain a metamagic wizard feat of your choice that has a level requirement of no more than half your level, which you can use until your next daily preparations.
name: Metamagical Experimentation

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane Thesis " pfs="Standard">[Spell Blending](/ArcaneThesis.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 206

You theorize that spell slots are a shorthand for an underlying energy that powers all spellcasting, and you've found a way to tinker with the hierarchy of spell slots, combining them to fuel more powerful spells.

 When you make your daily preparations, you can trade two spell slots of the same level for a bonus spell slot of up to 2 levels higher than the traded spell slots. You can exchange as many spell slots as you have available. Bonus spell slots must be of a level you can normally cast, and each bonus spell slot must be of a different spell level. You can also trade any spell slot for two additional cantrips, though you cannot trade more than one spell slot at a time for additional cantrips in this way.
name: Spell Blending

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane Thesis " pfs="Standard">[Spell Substitution](/ArcaneThesis.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 206

You don't accept the fact that once spells are prepared, they can't be changed until your next daily preparation, and you have uncovered a shortcut allowing you to substitute new spells for those you originally prepared.

 You can spend 10 minutes to empty one of your prepared spell slots and prepare a different spell from your spellbook in its place. If you are interrupted during such a swap, the original spell remains prepared and can still be cast. You can try again to swap out the spell later, but you must start the process over again.
name: Spell Substitution

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane Thesis " pfs="Standard">[Staff Nexus](/ArcaneThesis.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 142

Your thesis maintains that early and intense adoption of staves from the first days of study can create a symbiotic bond between spellcaster and staff, allowing them to create remarkable magic together. You've formed such a connection with a makeshift staff you built, and you are ready to infuse any staff you encounter with greater power.

 You begin play with a makeshift staff of your own invention. It contains one cantrip and one 1st-level spell, both from your spellbook, but it gains no charges normally during your preparations; you must expend a spell slot to grant it charges in the same way you would add additional charges to a [normal staff](/Rules.aspx?ID=748). You can [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) your makeshift staff into any other type of staff for the new staff's usual cost, adding the two spells you originally chose to the staff you Craft.

 At 8th level, you can expend two spells instead of one when preparing your staff, adding additional charges equal to the combined levels of the expended spells. At 16th level, you can expend up to a total of three spells to add charges to the staff, adding additional charges equal to the combined levels of all three spells.
name: Staff Nexus

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane Thesis " pfs="Standard">[Staff Nexus](/ArcaneThesis.aspx?ID=10)</title>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 195

Your thesis maintains that early and intense adoption of staves from the first days of study can create a symbiotic bond between spellcaster and staff, allowing them to create remarkable magic together. You've formed such a connection with a makeshift staff you built, and you are ready to infuse any staff you encounter with greater power.

 You begin play with a makeshift [staff](/Rules.aspx?ID=3211) of your own invention. It has the magical trait and contains one cantrip and one 1st-rank spell, both from your spellbook. During your daily preparations, you can expend one spell to grant the staff a number of charges equal to that spell's rank, which dissipate after 24 hours. While you are holding the staff, you can Cast the Spells it contains. The 1st-rank spell consumes 1 charge but the cantrip doesn't require charges. If your staff is destroyed, you can create a new one over the course of 1 hour, though it has no charges.

 During your daily preparations, you can merge your makeshift staff with another staff in your possession, adding the two chosen spells to it. This staff gains charges from preparing it and from expending spells. Magical staves are described on [the Staves page](/Rules.aspx?ID=3211) of _GM Core_.

 At 8th level, you can expend two spells instead of one when preparing any staff, adding additional charges equal to the combined ranks of the expended spells. At 16th level, you can expend up to a total of three spells to add charges to the staff, adding additional charges equal to the combined ranks of all three spells.
name: Staff Nexus
