markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deviant Ability Classification " pfs="Standard">[Dragon](/DeviantFeats.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 100

Deviant abilities often trace their origin to massive concentrations of energy, such as being at ground zero of a detonating magical artifact. These energies can linger within an individual to grant them deviations falling under the dragon classification—gouts of flame, electrical breath, and the like—projecting and absorbing it to great effect.

 When you gain a dragon-type deviation, choose a type of damage, from cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. All abilities you gain from this deviation deal this type of damage and add the corresponding trait to the ability. You also use this damage type for the damage and weakness from your backlash.

<title level="2">Feats</title>

<document level="3" id="feat-3780" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3781" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3782" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3783" />

<title level="2">Backlash: Energetic Meltdown</title>

As you channel energy through your deviant abilities, some of it turns back on you, spiking your body temperature or dancing across your skin.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mild Backlash** You take damage equal to your level as energy scours your body.

**Moderate Backlash** Energy wracks your body. After taking the damage, you gain weakness to the energy type of your backlash until your next daily preparations. The weakness is equal to one-third of your level. You also lose immunity to the energy type if you had it, until your next daily preparations.

**Severe Backlash** Whatever furnace or well within you that provides the energy for your deviation overloads completely, cascading out of you in an uncontrolled burst. You deal 1d6 damage per level to all creatures in a 30-foot emanation with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. You automatically take full damage without attempting a save.

</column>
name: Dragon
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 100
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deviant Ability Classification " pfs="Standard">[Troll](/DeviantFeats.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 101

Especially common when a deviant ability is born of scientific experimentation, the troll classification connotes improved physical abilities. While often thought of as biological mutation, clockwork implants and personal magical enhancements are equally likely mechanisms.

<title level="2">Feats</title>

<document level="3" id="feat-3784" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3785" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3786" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3787" />

<title level="2">Backlash: Strained Metabolism</title>

Your body's overclocked physiology requires larger-than- usual amounts of nourishment, leaving you weakened when it kicks in mid-combat.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mild Backlash** Your metabolism immediately burns through your body's energy stores, making you [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) until you take a bite of food (which usually requires 2 Interact actions: one to retrieve the food and another to consume it).

**Moderate Backlash** Ravenous hunger wracks your body. You are enfeebled 1, clumsy 1, and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) until you can eat a full meal, which requires at least 10 minutes.

**Severe Backlash** The strain on your body is truly incapacitating. You become [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) as nausea wracks you, and you are also enfeebled 1 and clumsy 1 until you can eat a full meal (which requires you to no longer be sickened). You are fatigued until you can get a full night's rest.

</column>
name: Troll
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 101
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deviant Ability Classification " pfs="Standard">[Wraith](/DeviantFeats.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 102

Wraith-type deviations often result from contact with otherworldly presences or artifacts, comprising a strange assortment of abilities that involve realms beyond our own.

<title level="2">Feats</title>

<document level="3" id="feat-3788" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3789" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3790" />

<document level="3" id="feat-3791" />

<title level="2">Backlash: Encroaching Presence</title>

Seeing and touching the beyond leaves you open to its alien influence, warping your perceptions and thoughts.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mild Backlash** Something inexplicable touches your mind, leaving you with lingering horror. You become [frightened 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).

**Moderate Backlash** Disorienting visions and figments cover your vision, as if you're seeing through another set of alien senses. For 10 minutes, all creatures become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to you as they are covered in visual distortions only you can see.

**Severe Backlash** Your life force is siphoned away as the presence tugs your soul to wherever it resides. You become [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) until your next daily preparations.

</column>
name: Wraith
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 102
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deviant Ability Classification " pfs="Limited">[Blight Soul](/DeviantFeats.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 74

Whether through planar exploration or unintended catastrophe, you’ve come into close contact with Ayrzul’s Blight and have been forever changed. As the Blight is a realm of invasive rot, pernicious decay, and deadly radiation, so too are you capable of wielding the powers of decay.

<title level="2">Feats</title>

<document level="3" id="feat-7626" />

<document level="3" id="feat-7627" />

<document level="3" id="feat-7628" />

<document level="3" id="feat-7629" />

<title level="2">Backlash: Fungal Rot</title>

As you use your powers, radioactive molds and fungi consume your flesh, making you look (and feel) like a rotting, walking corpse.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mild Backlash** You take damage equal to your level as your body decays.

**Moderate Backlash** You take damage equal to your level and become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2.

**Severe Backlash** You take damage equal to twice your level and become sickened 4. You can’t reduce the value of your sickened condition below 1 for 1 hour. 

</column>
name: Blight Soul
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 74
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deviant Ability Classification " pfs="Limited">[Verdant Core](/DeviantFeats.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 75

The Verdant Core deviation represents an abundance of vitality and plant life, likely caused by being implanted with supernatural seeds, contact with the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) or the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35), or powerful [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) rituals. Regardless of the source, you’re a walking garden with grass, flowers, and other small plants sprouting from your flesh. You have powers over plants, wood, and growth.

<title level="2">Feats</title>

<document level="3" id="feat-7630" />

<document level="3" id="feat-7631" />

<document level="3" id="feat-7632" />

<document level="3" id="feat-7633" />

<title level="2">Backlash: Life-Draining</title>

The flourishing plant life that covers your body is a part of you, but it draws nutrients from your blood, making you weaker the more the flowers grow. As the plants grow, you need to spend more and more time recuperating. Recuperating in the sunshine lowers this duration, as it feeds the flowers, speeding your recovery.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mild Backlash** You become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 as the plants feed off your life force. This condition lasts until you spend 3 consecutive actions to rest and do nothing.

**Moderate Backlash** You become drained 2 and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) until you spend 20 minutes recuperating by resting and doing nothing. If you spend this time basking in sunshine, it instead takes 10 minutes.

**Severe Backlash** You become drained 3 until you spend 2 hours napping. While napping, you’re [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and can’t take actions or engage in exploration activities, but a creature can wake you with a single action with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. If you spend this time napping in natural sunlight, it instead takes 1 hour. You’re fatigued until you can get a full night’s rest.

</column>
name: Verdant Core
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 75
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deviant Ability Classification " pfs="">[Flicker](/DeviantFeats.aspx?ID=6)</title>

**Source** [Gatewalkers Player's Guide (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=287) pg. 10

Whether due to an artificially accelerated metabolism or the lingering effects of temporal phenomena, the end result of the flicker classification is largely the same—abilities that result in the perception of higher- than-average speed, kinetic acuity, and reflexes.

<title level="2">Feats</title>

<document level="3" id="feat-8031" />

<document level="3" id="feat-8030" />

<document level="3" id="feat-8032" />

<document level="3" id="feat-8033" />

<title level="2">Backlash: Desynchronized Motions</title>

As your body speeds faster and faster, you risk falling out of sync with reality, as the world slows around you to the point that your interactions with it become strange and unpredictable.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mild Backlash** The friction of your motion through a slower world sets you alight. You take 2d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). At 5th level, and every 5 levels after, the persistent fire damage increases by 1d4. You take this persistent fire damage even if in a liquid environment, such as underwater, as your motion simply surrounds you in boiling water.

**Moderate Backlash** You fall out of phase with nearby matter, becoming trapped in the process. You take the mild backlash effects, catching on fire, and your legs also partially phase into the ground, making you [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81). You can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) against your deviation DC, or you can slowly extract yourself without attempting a check over the course of 1 minute.

**Severe Backlash** Your body accelerates to such a degree that your senses and impulses cannot keep up. You become trapped in your own mind for what seems like an eternity, taking 1d4 nonlethal mental damage for each level you have. When you return, you are [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 3 for 10 minutes, and then stupefied 1 until your next [daily preparation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575). Your body ages one year during this time.

</column>
name: Flicker
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Gatewalkers Player's Guide (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=287) pg. 10
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deviant Ability Classification " pfs="">[Leech](/DeviantFeats.aspx?ID=7)</title>

**Source** [Gatewalkers Player's Guide (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=287) pg. 11

While deviations are often thought of as a gained ability—increased muscle mass, a well of unstable power, new senses—it’s just as common that the events that cause a deviation result in loss. With a fundamental part of the subject’s self stripped away, an absence remains. Though generally fatal, these events can rarely result in leech-type classifications, characterized by abilities that draw in energy and matter in a futile attempt to fill the void within.

<title level="2">Feats</title>

<document level="3" id="feat-8035" />

<document level="3" id="feat-8034" />

<document level="3" id="feat-8036" />

<document level="3" id="feat-8037" />

<title level="2">Backlash: All-Consuming Hunger</title>

The emptiness within you is a hunger that pushes at your mind to fill it, causing you to fixate powerfully on a potential source of energy near you.

<column gap="tiny">

**Mild Backlash** You glimpse the thrum of energy—the heat of a body, the flickering of synapses, the light of a soul— in a beautiful, captivating dance. You become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with one creature you just affected with your deviant ability; if multiple creatures would qualify, the GM chooses. This fascination lasts for 1 minute or until the target uses a hostile action against you or your allies, as normal.

**Moderate Backlash** Your fascination with the target causes you to forget your allies. As mild backlash, but you don’t treat anyone as your ally (though they might still treat you as theirs), meaning that the fascination ends only if your target uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against you.

**Severe Backlash** Your mind is filled with the single, all-consuming desire to feed upon the target. You become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), except that your target is always the creature affected by your deviant ability, as long as you can perceive them.

</column>
name: Leech
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Gatewalkers Player's Guide (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=287) pg. 11
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Rogue Racket " pfs="Standard">[Ruffian](/Rackets.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 180

You prefer to strong-arm or intimidate others rather than rely on finesse or fancy tricks. You might be an enforcer for organized crime, a highway bandit, or a noble who bullies others with threats of your family's power. Or, you might use your skills on the up and up, in a private security detail or as a guard in a city with the right temperament.

 You use whatever tools you have at hand to get the job done. You can deal sneak attack damage with any simple weapon, in addition to the weapons listed in the sneak attack class feature. When you critically succeed at an attack roll using a simple weapon and the target has the [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) condition (unable to focus on defending itself), you also apply the [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) for the weapon you're wielding. You don't gain these benefits if the weapon has a damage die larger than d8 (after applying any abilities that alter its damage die size).

 You're trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) and medium armor. You can choose Strength as your key ability score. When you gain light armor expertise, you also gain expert proficiency in medium armor, and when you gain light armor mastery, you also gain master proficiency in medium armor.
name: Ruffian

<title level="1" right="Rogue Racket " pfs="Standard">[Scoundrel](/Rackets.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 180

You use fast-talking, flattery, and a silver tongue to avoid danger and escape tricky situations. You might be a grifter or con artist, traveling from place to place with a new story or scheme. Your racket is also ideal for certain reputable professions, like barrister, diplomat, or politician.

 When you successfully [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48), the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against melee attacks you attempt against it until the end of your next turn. On a critical success, the target is flat-footed against all melee attacks until the end of your next turn, not just yours.

 You’re trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6). You can choose Charisma as your key ability score.
name: Scoundrel

<title level="1" right="Rogue Racket " pfs="Standard">[Thief](/Rackets.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 180

Nothing beats the thrill of taking something that belongs to someone else, especially if you can do so completely unnoticed. You might be a pickpocket working the streets, a cat burglar sneaking through windows and escaping via rooftops, or a safecracker breaking into carefully guarded vaults. You might even work as a consultant, testing clients’ defenses by trying to steal something precious.

 When a fight breaks out, you prefer swift, lightweight weapons, and you strike where it hurts. When you make a melee Strike with a [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) weapon or finesse unarmed attack, you can add your Dexterity modifier to the damage roll instead of your Strength modifier.

 You are trained in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17).
name: Thief

<title level="1" right="Rogue Racket " pfs="Standard">[Eldritch Trickster](/Rackets.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 134

When it comes to a big heist, every rogue worth their salt knows that magic can throw meticulous plans into chaos, whether in the form of arcane wards or pesky divinations. You dabble in magic just enough to help handle these problems by jury-rigging spells and throwing a bit of your own magic into the mix. You might be a solitary thief, performing burglaries that can't be explained by skill alone, or a specialist called in by other rogues to deal with magical security measures. You might even be on the up-and-up, hired to test magical defenses or even build them yourself.

 Choose a [multiclass archetype](/Archetypes.aspx?Category=4) that has a basic, expert, and master spellcasting feat. You gain that archetype's dedication feat as a bonus feat even though you don't meet its level prerequisite, though you must meet its other prerequisites. For you, the [Magical Trickster](/Feats.aspx?ID=562) rogue feat has a prerequisite of 2nd level instead of 4th level. You can choose the spellcasting ability score for the multiclass archetype you chose as your key ability score.
name: Eldritch Trickster

<title level="1" right="Rogue Racket " pfs="Standard">[Mastermind](/Rackets.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 134

Where others might use sleight of hand or a silver tongue to achieve their objectives, you rely on your intellect to craft intricate schemes. You likely view your operations as a chess game, always planning 10 steps ahead where others might plan three. You might be a detective determined to solve crimes or a spymaster in the service of a powerful family or nation. If you operate outside the law, you might be an aspiring crime lord or information broker, excellent at directing others toward suitable jobs.

 If you successfully identify a creature using [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against your attacks until the start of your next turn; if you critically succeed, it's flat-footed against your attacks for 1 minute.

 You're trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) and one of the following skills of your choice: [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13). You can choose Intelligence as your key ability score.

You rely on your intellect to craft intricate schemes, planning 10 steps ahead where others might plan three. You might be a driven detective, a spymaster in the service of a powerful house, or an aspiring crime lord or information broker.

 If you successfully identify a creature using [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), that creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against your attacks until the start of your next turn; if you critically succeed, it's off-guard against your attacks for 1 minute.

 You're trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) and one of the following skills of your choice: [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13). You can choose Intelligence as your key attribute.

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 166 You prefer the direct approach: strong-arming or intimidation. You might be an enforcer for organized crime, a highway bandit, or a noble who bullies others with threats of your family's power.
You use whatever tools you have at hand to get the job done. You can deal sneak attack damage with any weapon, not just the weapons listed in the sneak attack class feature. This benefit doesn't apply to a simple weapon with a damage die greater than d8 or a martial or advanced weapon with a damage die greater than d6. (Apply any abilities that alter the damage die size first.) Critical hits you make with these weapons gain the weapon's [critical specialization benefit](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) if the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to you. You're trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) and medium armor. You can choose Strength as your key attribute. When you gain light armor expertise, you also gain expert proficiency in medium armor, and when you gain light armor mastery, you also gain master proficiency in medium armor.
name: Ruffian

<title level="1" right="Rogue Racket " pfs="Standard">[Scoundrel](/Rackets.aspx?ID=8)</title>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 166

You use fast-talking, flattery, and a silver tongue to avoid danger and escape tricky situations. You might be a grifter or con artist, or you might use your skills in certain reputable professions, like a barrister or diplomat

 When you successfully [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48), the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against your melee attacks until the end of your next turn. On a critical success, the target is off-guard against all melee attacks until the end of your next turn, not just yours. If you Feint while wielding an agile or finesse melee weapon, you can [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=87) immediately after the Feint as a free action.

 You’re trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6). You can choose Charisma as your key ability score.
name: Scoundrel

<title level="1" right="Rogue Racket " pfs="Standard">[Thief](/Rackets.aspx?ID=9)</title>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 167

Nothing beats the thrill of taking something that belongs to someone else, especially if you can do so completely unnoticed. You might be a pickpocket working the streets, a cat burglar sneaking through windows, or even a consultant, testing clients’ vaults for openings.

 When a fight breaks out, you prefer swift, lightweight weapons, and you strike where it hurts. When you attack with a [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) melee weapon or finesse melee unarmed attack, you can add your Dexterity modifier to damage rolls instead of your Strength modifier.

 You are trained in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17).
name: Thief

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Rogue Racket " pfs="Standard">[Avenger](/Rackets.aspx?ID=10)</title>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 58

This Racket can be taken by [Avengers](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=282).

Trained to be a precise but brutal weapon by the priests of your faith, you eschew complex trickery for swift and deadly efficiency. Subtlety and deception have their uses, but when a false smile fails, your blades have their own way of finding the truth.

You are trained in your [deity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2048)’s [favored weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=805). Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency with simple or martial weapons, you also gain that proficiency rank with your deity’s favored weapon.

You can deal sneak attack damage to your prey with your deity’s favored weapon, in addition to the weapons listed in the sneak attack class feature. When you critically succeed at an attack roll using your deity’s favored weapon and the target has the [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) condition, you also apply the weapon’s [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effect.

You’re trained in your deity’s divine skill and medium armor; if you’re already trained in your deity’s divine skill, you are instead trained in another skill of your choice. You can choose Strength as your key attribute modifier. When you gain light armor expertise, you also gain expert proficiency in medium armor, and when you gain light armor mastery, you also gain master proficiency in medium armor.
name: Avenger

<title level="1" right="Investigator Methodology " pfs="Standard">[Alchemical Sciences](/Methodologies.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 57

Your methodology emphasizes chemical and alchemical analysis, collecting information from unusual particles and fluids found on the scene. You possess enough alchemical know-how to whip up a few tinctures to help you with your cases.

 You're trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) and gain the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=752) skill feat. In addition, you gain a standard [formula book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=22) for free and learn the formulas for two additional common 1st-level alchemical items, which must be [elixirs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=8) or [tools](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=10). Each time you gain a level, you learn the formula for one common alchemical elixir or alchemical tool of any level of item you can create. 

During your daily preparations, you can create a number of versatile vials—alchemical concoctions that can quickly be turned into elixirs and tools— equal to your Intelligence modifier. You can use the Quick Tincture action to turn one of these vials into an elixir or alchemical tool for which you know the formula.

<document level="2" id="action-547" />
name: Alchemical Sciences

<title level="1" right="Investigator Methodology " pfs="Standard">[Empiricism](/Methodologies.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 57

Everything comes down to data. Calculating statistics, running numbers, and using inductive reasoning allows you to determine the most likely outcome of any scenario, and anything out of place draws your keen attention.

 You are trained in one Intelligence-based skill of your choice. You gain the [That's Odd](/Feats.aspx?ID=1449) investigator feat, and you gain the Expeditious Inspection free action.

<title level="1" right="Investigator Methodology " pfs="Standard">[Forensic Medicine](/Methodologies.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 58

You've learned that in most cases, especially murders, criminals tend to leave more evidence of the crime on the body than they'd like to believe. Information from bruising, bone fractures, blood spatters, and even the life cycle of carrion insects can provide valuable clues that help reconstruct the scene.

 You're trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) and gain the [Forensic Acumen](/Feats.aspx?ID=2128) and [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=760) skill feats. When you use Battle Medicine, on a success the target recovers additional Hit Points equal to your level, and the target becomes temporarily immune for only 1 hour, not 1 day.

<title level="1" right="Investigator Methodology " pfs="Standard">[Interrogation](/Methodologies.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 58

People can't help but trust you, whether through your inherent likableness or your firm insistence on sticking to the truth. You have a way about you that gets others talking, and you've developed interrogative techniques to help you get to the truth of your investigations.

 You are trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6). You gain the [No Cause for Alarm](/Feats.aspx?ID=2139) skill feat. You can also [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=544) over the course of a conversation rather than spending dedicated time looking into the lead, provided the conversation lasts 1 minute or longer. For example, while trying to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), you could pursue the creature you're conversing with as a lead, and you could Pursue a Lead about an object while someone is telling you information about that object. You also gain the Pointed Question action.

<document level="2" id="action-549" />

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 103

Your methodology emphasizes chemical and alchemical analysis, collecting information from unusual particles and fluids found on the scene. You possess enough alchemical know-how to whip up a few tinctures to help you with your cases.

You’re trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) and gain the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5117) skill feat. Each time you gain a level, you learn the formula for one common alchemical elixir or alchemical tool of any level of item you can create.

During your daily preparations, you can create a number of versatile vials equal to your Intelligence modifier. Statistics for versatile vials appear in the [alchemist](/Classes.aspx?ID=56) class.

You gain the Quick Tincture action, which you can use to turn one of these vials into an elixir or alchemical tool for which you know the formula. If you have the ability to create versatile vials from more than one source (such as the [alchemist multiclass archetype](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=228)), you can use any of your versatile vials for any ability you have that requires them.

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 97

Your muse is a mystery, driving you to uncover the hidden secrets of the multiverse. If your muse is a creature, it might be a [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50) or otherworldly being; if a deity, it might be [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10) or [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12). You gain the [Bardic Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=179) feat and add [_true strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) to your spell repertoire.

<title level="1" right="Bard Muse " pfs="Standard">[Maestro](/Muses.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 97

Your muse is a virtuoso, inspiring you to greater heights. If it's a creature, it might be a performance-loving creature such as a [choral angel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=14) or [lillend azata](/Monsters.aspx?ID=36); if a deity, it might be [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=17). As a bard with a maestro muse, you are an inspiration to your allies and confident of your musical and oratorical abilities. You gain the [Lingering Composition](/Feats.aspx?ID=180) feat and add [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=291) to your spell repertoire.

<title level="1" right="Bard Muse " pfs="Standard">[Polymath](/Muses.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 97

Your muse is a jack of all trades, flitting between skills and pursuits. If it's is a creature, it might be an eclectic creature like a [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69); if a deity, it might be [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5) or [Calistria](/Deities.aspx?ID=3). As a bard with a polymath muse, you are interested in a wide array of topics but rarely dedicated to any one, and you rarely make up your mind—you want to try everything. You gain the [Versatile Performance](/Feats.aspx?ID=182) feat and add [_unseen servant_](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) to your spell repertoire.

<title level="1" right="Bard Muse " pfs="Standard">[Warrior](/Muses.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 112

The battlefield is your stage, the clang of steel, your song. Your muse engages in countless battles, whether reveling in combat or resigned to its necessity. If your muse is a creature, it might be an otherworldly soldier, such as a [planetar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=544), [archon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=9), [cornugon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=482), or [purrodaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=596). If it's a deity, it might be [Gorum](/Deities.aspx?ID=7). As a bard with a warrior muse, you train for battle in addition to performance, and you prepare your allies for the dangers of battle. You might even wade into the thick of things with them. You gain the [Martial Performance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1633) feat and add [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) to your spell repertoire.

If your muse is an otherworldly creature, it might be a mysterious aeon or an occult dragon; if a deity, it might be [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10) or [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12). Art inspired by an enigma muse could be cryptic, eerie, or laden with speculation and conspiracy.
As a bard with the enigma muse, you support your allies by providing knowledge alongside inspiration and occult support. **Muse Feat** [Bardic Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=4573)

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 98 The battlefield is your stage, the clang of steel, your song. Your muse has seen countless battles, whether reveling in combat or resigned to its necessity. An individual soldier or general might inspire you, but so might a battlefield or weapon with a particularly profound history. If your muse is a creature, it might be an otherworldly soldier, such as an archon or devil soldier. If it's a deity, it might be [Gorum](/Deities.aspx?ID=7). Art inspired by a warrior muse is triumphant and strident, often detailing epic battles.
As a bard with a warrior muse, you train for battle in addition to performance, and you prepare your allies for the dangers of combat. You might even wade into the thick of things with them. **Muse Feat** [Martial Performance](/Feats.aspx?ID=4576)
**Muse Spell** [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524)

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 214

To advance a mature animal companion to a savage animal companion, increase its Strength modifier by 2 and its Dexterity, Constitution, and Wisdom modifiers by 1. It deals 3 additional damage with its unarmed attacks. Increase its proficiency rank in Athletics to expert. It also learns the advanced maneuver for its type. If your companion is Medium or smaller, it grows by one size. Its attacks become magical for the purpose of ignoring resistances.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [World Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=6) pg. 117

When you pick your animal companion, you can choose to have it be a megafauna animal companion. Megafauna animal companions are mostly similar to other animal companions of the same category, though they look different, and they have one difference in their upgrade path. If you take a feat that would normally allow you to choose to have your companion become a savage or nimble animal companion, a megafauna companion instead can choose to become a savage or indomitable companion. An indomitable companion increases its Constitution modifier by 2 and its Strength, Dexterity, and Wisdom modifiers by 1. It deals 3 additional damage with its unarmed attacks. Increase its proficiency rank in Athletics to expert and its proficiency rank in barding to expert. It also learns the advanced maneuver for its type. If your companion is Medium or smaller, it grows in size by one category. Its attacks count as magical for the purpose of ignoring resistances.
name: Indomitable

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Animal Companion Advanced Option " pfs="Standard">[Genie-Touched](/Companions.aspx?ID=4&Advanced=true)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 123

Xerbystes II has had his palace spellcasters work to develop ways to replicate the great powers of the genie-touched horses of Qadira. These spellcasters have a rudimentary method in place that can grant such power to any animal companion, infusing them with elemental power and granting them otherworldly insight. Xerbystes will sometimes gift these companions as a favor to others or have his spellcasters infuse the companions of trusted allies.

 If you take a feat that would normally allow you to choose to have a savage or nimble animal companion, you can instead choose to for it to become a genie-touched companion. When you choose the feat, select the type of genie whose power infuses your companion. The genies and their elemental traits are as follows: [djinni](/Monsters.aspx?ID=212) ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5)), [efreeti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=214) ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)), [marid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=215) ([water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)), or [shaitan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=213) ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)). A genie-touched companion increases its Wisdom modifier by 2 and its Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution modifiers by 1. It deals 3 additional damage with its unarmed attacks; this damage is fire damage for an efreet-touched companion or of the same type as the unarmed attack for the others. It gains resistance 5 to damage of a type depending on the genie: acid for djinni, fire for efreeti or marid, and electricity for shaitan. If the companion is djinni-touched or efreeti-touched, increase its proficiency rank in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) to expert; if it is marid-touched or shaitan-touched, increase its proficiency rank in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) to expert. It also learns the advanced maneuver for its type. Its attacks count as magical for the purpose of ignoring resistances and gain the genie's elemental trait.
name: Genie-Touched

Increase its proficiency rank in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) to expert. It also learns the advanced maneuver for its type.

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 39

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Laughing shadow magus gets time jump as their 11th level Studious Spell instead of the limited shift blame._

Magic is freeing, a means to your ends, and you can use it to go where you want, do as you please, and avoid the consequences. You are a laughing shadow of spell and blade, always one step ahead of your foes, always with a trick up your sleeve.

 While in [Arcane Cascade](/Actions.aspx?ID=756) stance, you gain a +5-foot status bonus to your Speeds, or a +10-foot bonus if you're unarmored. If you have a free hand while in the stance and are attacking a [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creature, you increase the extra damage to 3, to 5 if you have weapon specialization, or to 7 if you have greater weapon specialization. You must have your other hand completely free; the extra damage doesn't apply if you have a [free-hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=181) weapon or other item in that hand, even if you would normally be able to use the hand for other things.

<title level="1" right="Magus Hybrid Study " pfs="Standard">[Sparkling Targe](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 39

You've studied the applications of magic, training yourself to perform not just offensive maneuvers but defensive tactics as well. When magic flows through you, your shield can block impossible things, even a dragon's breath or a [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=180).

 You gain the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) general feat.

 When you're in [Arcane Cascade](/Actions.aspx?ID=756) stance with your shield raised, your circumstance bonus to AC from your shield also applies to your saves against spells and other magical effects. In addition, damage you take as a result of a spell or magical effect while you're in Arcane Cascade can trigger your Shield Block reaction, even if the damage isn't physical. When blocking damage in this way, increase your shield's Hardness by an amount equal to the extra damage from Arcane Cascade (typically 1, but 2 if you have weapon specialization, or 3 if you have greater weapon specialization). These benefits apply whether you're using an actual shield, the [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) spell, or something else that works like a shield (such as a raised tome if you have the [Raise a Tome](/Feats.aspx?ID=2848) feat).

**Studious Spell (11th)** [Warding Aggression](/Spells.aspx?ID=1029)

**Studious Spell (13th)** [Mountain Resilience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610)

</column>

When you use [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755), you can make a ranged weapon or ranged unarmed Strike, as long as the target is within the first range increment of your ranged weapon or ranged unarmed attack. <title level="1" right="Magus Hybrid Study " pfs="Standard">[Twisting Tree](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 40

The staff is perhaps one of the simplest of weapons, but this simplicity belies its elegance and versatility. To you, a staff is casting implement and martial weapon alike—the foundation of a fighting style.

 While you wield a staff in one hand, the staff adjusts in shape and weight, gaining the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) trait and increasing its damage die size to 1d6. While you wield a staff in both hands, it lengthens, twists, and reshapes, gaining the [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190), [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), and [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196) traits. While you're in [Arcane Cascade](/Actions.aspx?ID=756) stance, you can Interact or Release to change your grip on the weapon as a free action when you Strike with your staff, including Strikes made in a [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755). This happens before you roll your attack roll. You can also Interact to change your grip on the staff as a free action triggered at the end of your turn.

<title level="1" right="Magus Hybrid Study " pfs="Standard">[Aloof Firmament](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=7)</title>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 112

You're a philosopher of the sword who codifies your study of qinggong along principles arcane and martial. Your blade teaches morality and immortality, and you illustrate its lessons with an academician's rigor. Through your training, you've learned to move about with grace and create opportunity to strike foes.

 You gain the [Cat Fall](/Feats.aspx?ID=5131) general feat, though you benefit from it only as long as you aren't using a shield and are wearing light or no armor.

 While in Arcane Cascade stance, your steps become even lighter, giving you the benefits of the [Quick Jump](/Feats.aspx?ID=5196) general feat. When you [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) while in that stance, your movement doesn't trigger reactions. If you take one of these actions, the next Strike you make before the end of your turn with a one-handed weapon in the sword group has greater damage from Arcane Cascade. Increase the extra damage to 4; increase it to 6 if you have weapon specialization, or to 8 if you have greater weapon specialization. You must have your other hand completely free; the extra damage doesn't apply if you have a free-hand weapon or other item in that hand, even if you would normally be able to use the hand for other things.

<column gap="tiny">

**Conflux Spell** [Sky Laughs at Waves](/Spells.aspx?ID=2135)

**Studious Spell (11th)** [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753)

**Studious Spell (13th)** [Variable Gravity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1026)

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 112 You disdain others’ reliance on crude contrivances of iron, instead favoring the elegance of fabric, with thread and brocade your panoply. Your mastery of qinggong allows qi-infused fabric to tear flesh, bind blades, and pierce steel. You can create a special weapon, qi-infused fabric, by Interacting to grip a length of cloth of 1 Bulk or less and filling it with your qi. If you Interact to draw the object, you can do this as part of that action. For most unfurling brocade magi, this item is a scarf, sash, ribbon, or a similarly voluminous article of worn clothing, such as long sleeves. You can use the fabric as a [bladed scarf](/Weapons.aspx?ID=273). Unlike a normal bladed scarf, you have the option to wield the fabric in one hand instead of two. Its damage die is d4 if held in one hand. If you’re wearing handwraps of mighty blows, you can apply any of its runes to the fabric, provided those runes could be added to a bladed scarf. This begins when you wield the fabric and ends when you cease doing so. While in Arcane Cascade stance, the magical energy flowing through your fabric partially animates it to accomplish an even greater range of tricks.
  • If you critically succeed at an Athletics check to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), you can spend a single action immediately afterward to flick your fabric, causing the disarmed object to fall to the ground in your space rather than in your opponent’s.
  • Your fabric gains the [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619) trait. If you succeed at an Athletics check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) using your fabric, you can spend a single action immediately afterward to pull the creature 5 feet toward you.
  • When you attempt to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) a foe using your fabric, the target can be two sizes larger than you instead of one.
**Conflux Spell** [Home Among Mulberry Leaves](/Spells.aspx?ID=2134) **Studious Spell (7th)** [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=2045) **Studious Spell (11th)** [Whirling Scarves](/Spells.aspx?ID=593) **Studious Spell (13th)** [Planar Tether](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636)

<title level="1" right="Magus Hybrid Study " pfs="Standard">[Resurgent Maelstrom](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=9)</title>

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 82

While fate may conspire to strip you of your armaments, you’re never helpless. For you, anything can be a dangerous weapon, bolstered by the might of towering waves and crushing tides. You know these tools are transient, and so you think nothing of shattering and reforming them with your magic.

 Your attacks with [improvised weapons](/Rules.aspx?ID=2192) do not take the normal –2 item penalty. While you wield an improvised weapon, streams of water flow from your limbs to surround the weapon, granting it the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait. These improvised weapons have a minimum damage die of 1d6, or 1d4 if the improvised weapon is [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526). Effects that apply runes to your unarmed attacks, like [_runic body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657) and [_handwraps of mighty blows_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086), also apply those runes to your non-magical improvised weapons. The improvised weapon also gains an additional trait, depending on how many hands it takes to wield. A one- handed improvised weapon gains the [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544) trait; if it would already have the backstabber trait, double the amount of additional precision damage. A two- handed improvised weapon gains the [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611) trait; if it would already have the forceful trait, treat the number of weapon damage dice the weapon has as 1 higher for the purpose of calculating the additional damage.

 While you’re in Arcane Cascade stance, you can manipulate the water around your improvised weapon, making it cut like a knife or bash with the force of falling rocks. The weapon gains the versatile traits for the types of damage that it doesn’t normally deal. For instance, an improvised weapon that normally deals bludgeoning damage gains the [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) P and versatile S traits.

<column gap="tiny">

**Studious Spell (11th)** [Aqueous Orb](/Spells.aspx?ID=1443)

**Studious Spell (13th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732)

</column>

 When throwing an alchemical bomb with the [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) trait, you can deal splash damage to only your primary target instead of the usual splash area.

When using advanced alchemy to make bombs during your daily preparations, you can use a batch of reagents to create any three bombs instead of just two of the same bomb.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Perpetual Infusions</title>
Choose two 1st-level bombs.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Perpetual Potency</title>
Choose two 3rd-level or lower bombs.

<title level="2" right="Level 13">Greater Field Discovery</title>
You can increase the splash on your bombs to damage creatures within 10 feet, or 15 feet if you have [Expanded Splash](/Feats.aspx?ID=109).

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Perpetual Perfection</title>
Choose two 11th-level or lower bombs.

<title level="1" right="Alchemist Research Field " pfs="Standard">[Chirurgeon](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 73

You concentrate on healing others with alchemy. You start with the formulas for two common 1st-level [alchemical elixirs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=8) with the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) trait (like [lesser antidote](/Equipment.aspx?ID=80), [lesser antiplague](/Equipment.aspx?ID=81), or [minor elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91)), in addition to your other formulas.

 You can use your proficiency rank in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) for anything that requires a proficiency rank in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) (such as prerequisites), and use your Crafting modifier in place of your Medicine modifier for all Medicine checks.

<title level="2" right="Level 5">Field Discovery</title>
When using advanced alchemy to make elixirs with the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) trait during your daily preparations, you can create three elixirs with each batch of reagents instead of two.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Perpetual Infusions</title>
Choose two 1st-level elixirs with the healing trait. If a creature heals Hit Points from a perpetual infusion, that creature is temporarily immune to healing Hit Points from subsequent perpetual infusions for 10 minutes (but not immune to any other effects of those infusions).

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Perpetual Potency</title>
Choose two 6th-level or lower elixirs with
the healing trait.

<title level="2" right="Level 13">Greater Field Discovery</title>
When you use [Quick Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=667) to create any type of elixir of life, the creature drinking the elixir gains the maximum Hit Points possible for that elixir, instead of rolling to determine the number of Hit Points regained.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Perpetual Perfection</title>
Choose two 11th-level or lower elixirs with the healing trait.

<title level="1" right="Alchemist Research Field " pfs="Standard">[Mutagenist](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 73

You focus on bizarre mutagenic transformations that sacrifice one aspect of a creature's physical or psychological being in order to strengthen another. You start with the formulas for two common 1st-level [alchemical mutagens](/Traits.aspx?ID=116) in your formula book, in addition to your other formulas.

 You also gain the following free action.

<document level="2" id="action-340" />

<title level="2" right="Level 5">Field Discovery</title>
When using advanced alchemy to make mutagens during your daily preparations, you can use a batch of reagents to create any three mutagens instead of just two of the same mutagen.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Perpetual Infusions</title>
Choose two 1st-level mutagens.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Perpetual Potency</title>
Choose two 3rd-level or lower mutagens.

<title level="2" right="Level 13">Greater Field Discovery</title>
If you imbibe another mutagen while you are under the effects of a mutagen that you created, you can gain the benefits and the drawbacks of both mutagens at once, despite the fact that they both have the [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) trait and would not normally function together. If you come under the effects of any further mutagens while benefiting from two mutagens, you lose the benefit of one of the former mutagens of your choice, while retaining the drawbacks of all the mutagens. If you are under the effects of two mutagens and you come under the effect of a non-mutagen polymorph effect, you lose the benefits of the mutagens while retaining the drawbacks of both.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Perpetual Perfection</title>
Choose two 11th-level or lower mutagens.
name: Mutagenist

<title level="1" right="Alchemist Research Field " pfs="Standard">[Toxicologist](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 106

You specialize in toxins and venoms of all types. You start with the formulas for two common 1st-level [alchemical poisons](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=9) in your formula book, in addition to your other formulas. You can apply an [injury poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=97) you're holding to a weapon you're wielding as a single action, rather than as a 2-action activity, and you can change the DCs of your infused poisons to your class DC if it's higher.

<title level="2" right="Level 5">Field Discovery</title>
When using advanced alchemy to make poisons during your daily preparations, you can spend one batch of reagents to create three poisons in any combination, instead of just two of the same poison.

<title level="2" right="Level 7">Perpetual Infusions</title>
Choose two common 1st-level alchemical poisons.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Perpetual Potency</title>
Choose two common 5th-level or lower alchemical poisons.

<title level="2" right="Level 13">Greater Field Discovery</title>
You can apply two different injury poisons to the same weapon, though not to a piece of ammunition. The two poisons can be up to six levels lower than your level, and you can't use the poisons made without spending a batch of infused reagents via perpetual infusions. Applying the two poisons requires a separate action to apply each poison. Once completed, you combine the two poisons on the weapon into a double poison with the lower of the two poisons' DCs. This double poison is only [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) if both poisons were virulent, and if the poisons have a different number of stages, the double poison has a number of stages equal to the poison with the lower number of stages. The target takes the effects of both poisons for its current stage.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Perpetual Perfection</title>
Choose two common 11th-level or lower alchemical poisons.
name: Toxicologist

<title level="1" right="Alchemist Research Field " pfs="Standard">[Bomber](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=5)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 61

You specialize in explosions and other violent reactions.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Formulas</title>
Two common 1st-level [alchemical bombs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=7).

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Field Benefit</title>
When throwing an alchemical bomb with the [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) trait, you can choose to deal splash damage to only your primary target instead of the usual splash area.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Field Vials</title>
When you Strike with a versatile vial, you can choose to have it deal cold, electricity, or fire damage instead of acid damage.

<title level="2" right="Level 5">Field Discovery</title>
You have calculated all the angles to maximize a bomb’s splash. When you throw an alchemical bomb with the splash trait, you can cause the bomb to deal splash damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1) instead of the normal amount.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Advanced Vials</title>
You can mix special ingredients into your vials, such as magical dust or flakes of precious minerals. When you create a versatile vial, you can choose to have it treated as one special material of your choice, chosen from [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915), [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916), and [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917). If you’re wearing or wielding an item of a different precious material when you make the vial, you can choose that material instead.

<title level="2" right="Level 13">Greater Field Discovery</title>
You can increase the splash on your bombs to damage creatures within 10 feet, or 15 feet if you have [Expanded Splash](/Feats.aspx?ID=5788).
name: Bomber

<title level="1" right="Alchemist Research Field " pfs="Standard">[Chirurgeon](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=6)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 61

You concentrate on healing others with alchemy.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Formulas</title>
Two common 1st-level [alchemical elixirs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=8) with the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) trait (like [lesser antidote](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2879), [lesser antiplague](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2880), or [minor elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2887)).

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Field Benefit</title>
You can use your proficiency rank in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) for anything that requires a proficiency rank in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) (such as prerequisites) and use your Crafting modifier in place of your Medicine modifier for all Medicine checks.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Field Vials</title>
Your versatile vials can be used to heal a living creature a number of Hit Points equal to the vial’s initial damage. A creature can drink the vial for this benefit, or you can throw the vial at a willing creature within 20 feet as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to heal that creature. In either case, a vial used this way loses the [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) traits and gains the [coagulant](/Traits.aspx?ID=796) and [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) traits, plus the [elixir](/Traits.aspx?ID=589) trait if a creature drinks it.

<title level="2" right="Level 5">Field Discovery</title>
Your medicinal elixirs are quite fortifying. When a creature drinks an [infused](/Traits.aspx?ID=797) elixir with the healing trait that you have created, that creature gains a number of [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 0); these temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Advanced Vials</title>
When you use a field vial to heal a creature that has half its maximum Hit Points or fewer, the [coagulant](/Traits.aspx?ID=796) trait doesn’t apply to that healing.

<title level="2" right="Level 13">Greater Field Discovery</title>
When you use [Quick Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=2801) to create any type of elixir of life, the creature healed by the item regains the maximum Hit Points possible, instead of rolling to determine the number of Hit Points regained.
name: Chirurgeon

<title level="1" right="Alchemist Research Field " pfs="Standard">[Mutagenist](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=7)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 61

You focus on bizarre mutagenic transformations that sacrifice one aspect of a creature’s physical or psychological being in order to strengthen another.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Formulas</title>
Two common 1st-level [alchemical mutagens](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=8).

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Field Benefit</title>
When you use a [mutagen](/Traits.aspx?ID=808), you gain a number of [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 0) plus half your level. These temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute or until the mutagen’s duration expires, whichever comes first. You can’t gain temporary HP from drinking a mutagen again for 1 minute.

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Field Vials</title>
You can drink the contents of one your versatile vials to suppress the drawback you take from one mutagen currently affecting you for 1 minute. A vial used this way loses the [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [bomb](/Traits.aspx?ID=548), and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) traits and gains the [elixir](/Traits.aspx?ID=589) trait. If you have more than one drawback due to [Combine Elixirs](/Feats.aspx?ID=5777) or a similar ability, drinking the vial suppresses one drawback of your choice.

<title level="2" right="Level 5">Field Discovery</title>
The mutagens flowing through your blood have altered your body chemistry. When you roll a Fortitude save while affected by a mutagen and dislike the result, you can end the effect of the mutagen to reroll the save. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Advanced Vials</title>
When you drink one of your field vials, you also gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to physical damage for the same duration. The resistance is equal to half your level.

<title level="2" right="Level 13">Greater Field Discovery</title>
If you imbibe another mutagen while you are under the effects of a mutagen, you can gain the benefits and the drawbacks of both mutagens at once, despite the fact that they both have the [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) trait and would not normally function together. If you come under the effects of any further mutagens while benefiting from two mutagens, you lose the benefit of one of the former mutagens of your choice, while retaining the drawbacks of all the mutagens. If you are under the effects of two mutagens and you come under the effect of a non-mutagen polymorph effect, you lose the benefits of the mutagens while retaining the drawbacks of both. Your field vial benefit applies to one mutagen of your choice.
name: Mutagenist

<title level="1" right="Alchemist Research Field " pfs="Standard">[Toxicologist](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=8)</title>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 62

You specialize in toxins and venoms of all types. 

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Formulas</title>
Two common 1st-level [alchemical poisons](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=9).

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Field Benefit</title>
You can Activate an [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=635) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) as a single action, provided you apply it to a weapon you're holding or a piece of ammunition on your person or loaded in a weapon you're holding. This can be an item you're holding or you can Interact to draw an eligible item or make one using Quick Alchemy as part of the single action; if you can make more than one item with Quick Alchemy (such as with the double brew class feature), you can still Activate only one.

 In addition, you flexibly mix acidic and poisonous alchemical compounds. Your [infused](/Traits.aspx?ID=797) items with the poison trait can affect creatures [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) to poison. A creature takes acid damage instead of poison damage from such items if either the creature is immune to poison or that would be more detrimental to the creature (as determined by the GM).

<title level="2" right="Level 1">Field Vials</title>
Your versatile vials have the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) trait and deal poison damage instead of having the [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) trait and dealing acid damage (though your field benefit still applies). You can apply the contents of a versatile vial to a weapon or piece of ammunition as an [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=635) poison. Add the versatile vial’s initial damage to the first successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with that weapon or ammunition. The substance becomes inert at the end of your current turn.

<title level="2" right="Level 5">Field Discovery</title>
You have handled enough poisons to become inured to their effects. You gain poison [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) equal to half your level.

<title level="2" right="Level 11">Advanced Vials</title>
When you damage a creature with a versatile vial you’ve used as an [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=635) poison, that creature takes [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) equal to the vial’s splash damage in addition to the initial damage.

<title level="2" right="Level 13">Greater Field Discovery</title>
When a creature fails its initial saving throw against an [infused](/Traits.aspx?ID=797) [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=635) poison you created, the wound sprays poison onto another creature adjacent to it. The attacker who caused the injury chooses which creature, if there’s more than one, and can choose to forgo this effect. That creature is exposed to the poison. The second creature doesn’t spread the poison further.
name: Toxicologist

<title level="1" right="Psychic Subconscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[Emotional Acceptance](/SubconsciousMinds.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 15

The mind's truths come not in learned words or mathematical formulas but in deeper feelings and sensations. Emotions unlock the door to your power—you might consider your heart the source of your strength, or you might find that the infinite complexity felt in a moment expresses your power more efficiently than any string of mystic words ever could.

 Your thought components are emotions. You might summon the sense of freedom to buoy you through the air or let fear grip your heart even as you plant it in another's mind. Emotion components tend to impart abstract or vivid effects to your visual and auditory spell manifestations as well as more intangible flashes of emotion, such as swirls of joyous color or a sense of growing melancholy.

<column gap="tiny">

**Key Attribute** Your key attribute is Charisma.

**Psyche Action** [Restore the Mind](/Actions.aspx?ID=1223)

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-1223" />

<title level="1" right="Psychic Subconscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[Gathered Lore](/SubconsciousMinds.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 15

Many psychics are self-taught, frantically improvising how to best control their abilities before their power overwhelms them. Not you. Tutored by a mentor or classically trained at a facility for psychic development, you've learned techniques and teachings for best harnessing the mind, cataloging each expression of psychic power with a specific teaching, anecdote, or phrase.

 Your thought components are mantras you associate with a given spell, which you mentally repeat as you cast. You might silently utter a teaching of resilience as you spin force into a barrier or hear the first three notes of a traveling song as you slip through space. Mantra components often produce runes or symbols from your learnings that are unique to each spell you cast, causing your spell manifestations to resemble those of conventional spellcasters to a much greater degree than those of other psychics.

<column gap="tiny">

**Key Attribute** Your key attribute is Intelligence.

**Psyche Action** [Recall the Teachings](/Actions.aspx?ID=1224)

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-1224" />

<title level="1" right="Psychic Subconscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[Precise Discipline](/SubconsciousMinds.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 16

Determine intended outcome. Align thoughts. Identify and resolve distractions. Manifest. Intentional focus is the key to unleashing your abilities, whether that's because your power naturally tends to precise, discrete effects, or because you've adopted strict discipline to ensure your mind can change the world only when you intend it to.

 Your thought components are calculations you make to determine the forces you need to call upon. Perhaps you mentally weigh the mass of an object against the force of gravity to levitate it or you might determine exactly how much energy is needed for an object to combust. Calculation components tend to impart ordered visual and auditory effects to your spell manifestation, such as regular tessellations of light, mathematical spirals, or harmonic tones.

<column gap="tiny">

**Key Attribute** Your key attribute is Intelligence.

**Psyche Action** [Calculate Threats](/Actions.aspx?ID=1225)

</column>

<title level="1" right="Psychic Subconscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[Wandering Reverie](/SubconsciousMinds.aspx?ID=4)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 16

The subconscious is by definition buried below the surface. Thus, only by stripping away the clamor and interference of everyday thought can you bring this more fundamental part of your mind to bear. Whether through sleep, self-hypnosis, or special exercises to empty your mind, you've found a way to access a deeper, more chaotic state of free association that better channels your psychic power.

 Your thought components come from your imagination, images, and sensations your mind freely associates. You might have a passing daydream of small creatures bringing you an object as you telekinetically attract it or you may blink to find you've teleported to your desired location. Spell manifestations from imagination components have surreal or indistinct qualities, and the exact details fade quickly from observers' memory, as sensations from a dream.

<column gap="tiny">

**Key Attribute** Your key attribute is Charisma.

**Psyche Action** [Fade into Daydreams](/Actions.aspx?ID=1226)

</column>

 Your thoughtforms are mantras you associate with a given spell, which you mentally repeat as you cast. You might silently utter a teaching of resilience as you spin force into a barrier or hear the first three notes of a traveling song as you slip through space. Mantra thoughtforms often produce runes or symbols from your learnings that are unique to each spell you cast, causing your spell manifestations to resemble those of conventional spellcasters to a much greater degree than those of other psychics.

<column gap="tiny">

**Key Attribute** Your key attribute is Intelligence.

**Psyche Action** [Recall the Teachings](/Actions.aspx?ID=3540)

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Subconscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[Precise Discipline](/SubconsciousMinds.aspx?ID=7)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 16

Determine intended outcome. Align thoughts. Identify and resolve distractions. Manifest. Intentional focus is the key to unleashing your abilities, whether that's because your power naturally tends to precise, discrete effects, or because you've adopted strict discipline to ensure your mind can change the world only when you intend it to.

 Your thoughtforms are calculations you make to determine the forces you need to call upon. Perhaps you mentally weigh the mass of an object against the force of gravity to levitate it or you might determine exactly how much energy is needed for an object to combust. Calculation thoughtforms tend to impart ordered visual and auditory effects to your spell manifestation, such as regular tessellations of light, mathematical spirals, or harmonic tones.

<column gap="tiny">

**Key Attribute** Your key attribute is Intelligence.

**Psyche Action** [Calculate Threats](/Actions.aspx?ID=3541)

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-3541" />

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Psychic Subconscious Mind " pfs="Standard">[Wandering Reverie](/SubconsciousMinds.aspx?ID=8)</title>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 16

The subconscious is by definition buried below the surface. Thus, only by stripping away the clamor and interference of everyday thought can you bring this more fundamental part of your mind to bear. Whether through sleep, self-hypnosis, or special exercises to empty your mind, you've found a way to access a deeper, more chaotic state of free association that better channels your psychic power.

 Your thoughtforms come from your imagination, images, and sensations your mind freely associates. You might have a passing daydream of small creatures bringing you an object as you telekinetically attract it or you may blink to find you've teleported to your desired location. Spell manifestations from imagination thoughtforms have surreal or indistinct qualities, and the exact details fade quickly from observers' memory, as sensations from a dream.

<column gap="tiny">

**Key Attribute** Your key attribute is Charisma.

**Psyche Action** [Fade into Daydreams](/Actions.aspx?ID=3542)

</column>

<document level="2" id="action-3542" />

<title level="1" right="Ranger Hunter's Edge " pfs="Standard">[Flurry](/HuntersEdge.aspx?ID=1)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 168

You have trained to unleash a devastating flurry of attacks upon your prey. Your multiple attack penalty for attacks against your hunted prey is –3 (–2 with an agile weapon) on your second attack of the turn instead of –5, and –6 (–4 with an agile weapon) on your third or subsequent attack of the turn, instead of –10.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Masterful Hunter</title>

You can blend your weapon mastery with skillful targeting to make a series of precise attacks. If you have master proficiency with your weapon, your multiple attack penalty for attacks against your hunted prey is –2 (–1 with an agile weapon) on your second attack of the turn, and –4 (–2 with an agile weapon) on your third and subsequent attacks of the turn.
name: Flurry

<title level="1" right="Ranger Hunter's Edge " pfs="Standard">[Precision](/HuntersEdge.aspx?ID=2)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 168

You have trained to aim for your prey’s weak points. The first time you hit your hunted prey in a round, you also deal 1d8 additional precision damage. (Precision damage increases the damage you already deal, using the same type, but is ineffective against creatures that lack vital organs or weak points.) At 11th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8 precision damage, and at 19th level, the extra damage increases to 3d8 precision damage.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Masterful Hunter</title>

Your weapon mastery allows you to hit your prey’s vital areas multiple times. The second time in a round you hit your hunted prey, you also deal 1d8 precision damage. At 19th level, your second hit in a round against your hunted prey deals 2d8 precision damage, and your third hit in a round against your hunted prey deals 1d8 precision damage.
name: Precision

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Ranger Hunter's Edge " pfs="Standard">[Outwit](/HuntersEdge.aspx?ID=3)</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 168

You are talented at outwitting and evading your prey. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks, and any checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about the prey, and a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against your prey's attacks.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Masterful Hunter</title>

Your mastery of skills allows you to overwhelm your prey. If you have master proficiency in Deception, Intimidation, Stealth, or the skill you use to Recall Knowledge about your prey, increase the circumstance bonus against the prey with that skill from +2 to +4. If you have master proficiency with your armor, increase the circumstance bonus to AC against the prey from +1 to +2.
name: Outwit

<title level="1" right="Ranger Hunter's Edge " pfs="Standard">[Vindication](/HuntersEdge.aspx?ID=7)</title>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 64

This Hunter's Edge can be taken by [Vindicators](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=285).

Your specialized training gives you a unique edge that allows you to more effectively bring the tools of your faith to bear against the monsters that lurk in the night or hide among the faithful.

You gain a +1 status bonus to your spell attack rolls against your [hunted prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=2257), and they take a –1 status penalty to their saving throws against [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spells you cast. You gain the [_vindicator’s mark_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2148) warden spell.

<title level="2" right="Level 17">Masterful Hunter</title>

You combine skill and magic to swiftly defeat your prey. The status bonus to divine spell attack rolls against your hunted prey increases to +2, and the status penalty to their saving throws against divine spells you cast is –2.
name: Vindication
